observations.recommendation: That the City Government immediately negotiate with Squidpay for an SP agreement regarding a collection and settlement procedure with the incorporation of a partner bank as required under Section VII of DTI-DOF JDAO No. 02, S. 2006. To avoid further complications, temporary discontinuance of the use of the Electronic Payment Collection System provided by the Electronic Payment Gateway Provider - Squidpay Technology, Inc., is also recommended until such time that the deficiencies are sufficiently addressed.
observations.managementAction:
The matter is referred to the SP and is currently pending with the Committee on Laws, Human Rights and Justice for study. CTO undertakes to resolve the issue.
The matter is referred to the SP and is currently pending with the Committee on Laws, Human Rights and Justice for study. CTO undertakes to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: That the City Government initiate the amendment of the conditions of the MOA with the EPGP – Squidpay Technologies, Inc., to incorporate the basic Principles of Implementation of an EPCS, and to include the provisions on the minimum requirements provided under Section VII of DTI-DOF JDAO No. 02, S. 2006.
observations.managementAction: The matter is referred to the SP and is currently pending with the Committee on Laws, Human Rights and Justice for study. CTO undertakes to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation:
a. Remit to the Bureau of Treasury the balance of the unremitted collections for Fire Code Fees as of December 31, 2020 amounting to ₱21,317,645.24; and
b. Comply with the terms of the MOA between the City Government of Baguio and the BFP, specifically to remit/deposit the monthly collection on or before the fifteenth (15th) day of the following month in the Bureau of Fire Protection Landbank Account Name: Bureau of Treasury – CAR No. 3402280416 (Naguilian Branch).

a. That the City Government, through the City Treasurer, after depositing all unremitted collections of fire code fees, coordinate with the BFP-Baguio City to compute the LGU share of the City from the total fire code fees collected by both LGU and BFP; and
b. Coordinate with the BFP and the Department of Budget and Management for the release of the SARO and NCA corresponding to the computed 20% share of the City Government from all fire code fees remitted from CY 2010 to CY 2020.
observations.managementAction: As per AAPSI dated September 15, 2021, the CTO undertakes to demand the latest computations from the Bureau of Fire Protection. Management already made the proper coordination with BFP and DBM for the release of the SARO and NCA corresponding to the computed 20% share of the City Government from all fire code fees remitted from CY 2010 to 2020.

observations.recommendation:
a. That the City Government handle its cash through the imprest system whereby payments (to suppliers) are to be made by check or through Automatic Debit to Account, or in instances when it may be very difficult, impractical or impossible to make payments by check, payments may be made by the disbursing officer in the form of cash through cash advance drawn and maintained in accordance with COA rules and regulation; and
b. Require the supplier JP Acosta Trading, through the GSO, a Sales Invoice as acknowledgment of receipt of payment for the goods/medical supplies by the City Government, and a legally acceptable document that would justify the deposit of the cash payment to Rigging Trading instead of to J.P. Acosta.

That Management, henceforth, prepare the required Quarterly: (1) Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for In-kind Donations; (2) Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations; (3) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated Relief Goods; (4) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated PPE; and furnish the same to the NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the Audit Team within ten working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-009 in relation to COA Circular No. 2014-002.

That Management prepare and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers (SL) for Trust Liabilities – DRRMF for all cash donations received amounting to ₱100,000.00 and above per donor per purpose while those below ₱100,000 donations shall be grouped per purpose and posted in a separate SL appropriately labeled as “Various Donors” in order to facilitate proper accounting and monitoring of donations as prescribed by COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

a. That the City Accountant, take up the following journal entries in the books of accounts to adjust the overstated net book value of the Property Plant and Equipment account and the corresponding understated balance of the Government Equity account; and
b. Adopt the Enhanced Electronic National Government Accounting System (eNGAS) and eBudget Version 2.0 for Local Government Units which are PPSAS (IPSAS) and RCA compliant.
observations.managementAction: The CAO agreed to take up the journal entries for the adjustments to reflect the proper values of the assets. Roll-out of eNGAS Ver. 2.0 was made to the COA Central Office in 2017. Despite periodic follow-ups, the request is still pending with COA Central Office to date.

observations.recommendation:
a. That the City Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the items previously recognized as PPE to their proper Inventory accounts, and recognize the corresponding expense.
b. That the General Services Office to issue Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) instead of Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) or the Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR), to establish accountability over the items issued.
observations.managementAction: The CAO stated that reconciliation and adjustments for SEF asset items is ongoing while GF asset items are fully adjusted. In addition, the GSO has prepared Inventory Custodian Slips for 50% of the items with individual costs below the capitalization threshold and some items were dropped from the GSO's Annual Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

observations.recommendation:
a. That the General Service Office, initiate the process of disposing the unserviceable properties in accordance with the modes of disposal/divestment of assets and property recognized under COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989; and
b. That the City Accountant, record the eventual disposal of unserviceable properties following the appropriate journal entry recommended under section 267 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: For the year 2020, the City General Services Office was able to conduct public auctions of unserviceable properties on November 19 and December 11, 2020. Request letters to CAO dated January 12, 2021 (2 letters) and February 01, 2021 were made for the dropping of some of the sold properties from the records of CAO. The corresponding journal entries have been prepared by CAO as adjustments for these PPEs: -JEV-2021-04-004491 -JEV-20221-04-004760 -JEV-20221-03-001339 -JEV-2021-05-003118 (GF) -JEV-2021-05-003391 (LDF) -JEV-2021-05-007126 -JEV-2021-05-007408 -JEV-2021-05-007419

The corresponding journal entries have been prepared by CAO as adjustments for CIP: -JEV-2021-01-000513 -JEV-2021-03-001338 -JEV-2021-03-001339 -JEV-2021-05-003118 -JEV-2021-05-003391 Recording of CIP is at Contract Amount upon first billing.

observations.recommendation:
a. Review the unimplemented GAD PPAs if these are still feasible and update and implement the same to maximize the utilization of GAD funds in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided; and
b. Submit within the required schedule - the PCW-endorsed annual GAD Plan and Budget which is within five (5) working days from endorsement, and the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report as required in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 so as to facilitate verification and audit.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management exhaust all available remedies provided under the laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance for the settlement of the Charges and for the Disallowances without pending appeal to COA as provided in the RRSA.

That the Management to require the concerned offices to submit the aforementioned relevant documents to substantiate the recorded adjusting entry and to establish the validity, regularity and propriety of the subject adjustment on the Land account.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO stated that aside from the regular functions such as research and conduct of surveys of parcels of land for titling in the name of the City, indemnity of City owned buildings and facilities, and inventory and inspections of buildings/ structures, two (2) personnel in the Real Property Management Section were also requested by the barangay officials to conduct surveys/ inspection in their barangays.
In response to the letter dated August 26, 2020, the City Planning and Development Office has submitted Index Maps subject for inspection and verification.

observations.recommendation:
a. That the management to conduct an inventory of work accomplished by the contractor and impose liquidated damages at least equal to one-tenth of one percent (.01%) of the cost of the unperformed portion for every day of delay as required under Section 68 RA 9184;
b. That the management to require the contractor to engage a catch-up program in order to fast track the completion of the project to avoid termination of then contract as stipulated also under section 68 of RA 9184;
observations.managementAction:
As of December 31, 2020, the OIC- Department Head of the Buildings and Architecture Office in reply to the observation stated that the project will be subjected to liquidated damages upon final billing.
As of December 31, 2020, a Fast Track Order was issued to the Contractor and then Show Cause Order was consequently issued after increase of negative slippage.

observations.recommendation:
a. That the management advise the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to intensify the conduct of verification and validation during Post-Qualification, such as in checking the performance of a bidder in its on-going government and private contracts;
b. That the management avoid awarding projects to bidders who have on-going projects with negative slippages of at least fifteen percent (15%) in any of one project or a negative slippage of at least ten percent (10%) in each of its two or more contracts as required under Rule X - Post-Qualification of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction:
As of December 31, 2020, the OIC- Department Head of the Buildings and Architecture Office in reply to the observation stated that the project will be subjected to liquidated damages upon final billing. A Fast Track Order was issued to the Contractor and Show Cause Order was consequently issued after increase of negative slippage.
The management advised the BAC and TWG to intensify verification and validation during post-qualification as recommended.

observations.recommendation: That the management review and allocate reasonable cost-effective budgets for the Plans, Programs and Activities (PPAs) in the LDRRMP in order to avoid the under/over budgeting of resources that could have been allocated to other PPAs;

That the management monitor the delinquencies as reflected in the monthly report of stall rentals, intensify the collection thereof and avail of other available remedies to collect from non-compliant and delinquent lessees.
observations.managementAction: The CTO stated that they shall continue with the usual way of collection, that is, issue collection/demand letters. When the pandemic is over, and when business has rebounded, the CTO will do the intensification of the collection and the enforcement of penalties imposed by laws.

observations.recommendation: That the management revisit the AOMs mentioned above, which were previously issued and discussed with management and initiate appropriate action and the Baguio Lions Club Deed of Usufruct between the City of Baguio is still pending with the Committee on Laws, Human Rights and Justice of the Sangguniang Panlungsod. The proposed terms and conditions for renting the lot where the club building is constructed is still being reviewed.
observations.managementAction: As of September 2021, the management updated that Deed of Usufruct between the City of Baguio and the Baguio Lions Club is still pending with the Committee on Laws, Human Rights and Justice of the Sangguniang Panlungsod. The proposed terms and conditions for renting the lot where the club building is constructed is still being reviewed.

observations.recommendation: That the City General Services Officer and the City Engineer coordinate and facilitate the immediate preparation of the City’s Local Road Inventory Report and the corresponding Road Map in accordance with the costing and timelines provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008 and to submit the same to the City Accountant for proper recording.
observations.managementAction: The GSO stated that the Road Map was already prepared 100%, however the inspection, appraisal and update of the road map and road inventory will be conducted and updated as a prerequisite in the preparation of the City’s Local Road Inventory Report. In addition, GSO said that the delay in the implementation is due to Covid pandemic lockdown and restrictions and lack of technical personnel to conduct site inspection and appraisal.

observations.recommendation: Demand the immediate payment of due and demandable receivables and henceforth implement the repayment scheme guidelines for all loans;
observations.managementAction: The CSWDO updated that they continuously issue demand letters for payment and increase the number of bonded employees to assist in the collection at the barangay level. They also said that collections of demandable loans have taken a low turnout since most of the beneficiaries with outstanding loan have been affected by the impact of Covid-19.

observations.recommendation: The amount of ₱2 million in the 20% Local Development Fund but did not qualify as a development project be reverted to the unappropriated surplus. The same to be programmed for projects that contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environment management in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1. Funds for said renovation shall then be sourced from the General Fund of the City.

Immediately secure the Deed of Donation and the acquisition cost or appraised value of the donated properties and furnish a copy thereof to the CAO for proper recording in the properties per COA guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The GSO inspected the properties on May 3, 2021 but the Deed of Donation was not available. Thus, the GSO will appraise the properties and will furnish to CAO as per COA guidelines.

observations.recommendation: Ensure strict compliance with the provisions on the concurrence of the SP as a measure of check and balance on the authority of the Local Chief Executive to enter into contracts and to ensure that the use of resources and revenues for the development plans and programs for the BCC are maximized.
observations.managementAction: The CLO updated that there is only a single lessee at the Baguio Convention Center, that is, Family Mart and as per their record, there are no MOAs or contracts entered into by the City with BCC that is pending with them. As per their information from City Tourism Office / CADMO, the MOA is still with the City Council.

observations.recommendation: Enforce the collection of unpaid rent totaling ₱130,000.00 from Glorious Freedom Community Church and CMCI.
observations.managementAction: The last update from the CADMO regarding this matter was last December 31, 2020 and they stated that CMCI vacated the space in June 2016 but they have yet to settle their remaining balance of ₱20,000.00. All efforts were exhausted to communicate with CMCI but it does not hold office and is no longer active in Baguio City. Legal remedies will be initiated to recover the said amount. The CLO commented that there is no formal request from the CADMO/Tourism Office to institute legal remedies to recover the said amount but they don’t recommend doing so since the legal expenses that the City will incur may be much higher than the amount sought to be recovered.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned members of the SP for the immediate refund of the overpaid RATA.
observations.managementAction: The CAO has partially collected the overpayment of RATA. As of December 31, 2021, the balance of the unrefunded RATA amounts to ₱25,500.00.

observations.recommendation:
a. Require incumbent barangay officials who are in the list of those who have not yet settled their long outstanding obligations relative to the trip in 2003 to Mandaue City, Cebu, to settle their accounts in full or through payroll deduction from their honoraria. If warranted and in case they refuse to pay, coordinate with the City Legal Office to enforce payment thereof; and
b. Issue demand letters and exert more effort to collect payments from those who are no longer in the service and, if all remedies for collection have been made but to no avail, consider write-off requesting from the Commission on Audit for the write-off of the long outstanding receivables pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 duly supported with documents to prove that collection is no longer possible.
observations.managementAction: The CAO stated that collection will still be enforced. During the monthly meetings of Punong Barangays, the CAO has made repeated demands to the incumbent barangay officials on the list to settle their dues. The CAO stated that the receivables were subjected to impairment in March 2021 and a request for write-off will be made to COA.

observations.recommendation: Determine actual areas being occupied by the two restaurants and submit a copy of the results of the survey to the Audit Team for verification purposes. Should it be established that the actual areas occupied/used by the two establishments are more than 212.15 square meters and 235 square meters, respectively, and then collect the amounting deficiency in rental income from 1999 to the present.
observations.managementAction: The CTO said that Ms. Rita Santos, the only delinquent lessee of the City has agreed to pay in installment after they have referred the same to the Legal Office. Moreover, the CTO said that although the Contract of Lease has already expired in October 2021 the Sangguniang Panlungsod enacted Resolution No. 557, s. 2021, entitled, “According/Observing Status Quo Ante All Burnham Park Lease Contracts or Month-To-Month Extension until the Final Burnham Park Development Plan is Ready for Execution”.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Office of the City Treasurer to intensify its efforts in the collection of delinquent lease rentals, including lot surcharges and interest. observations.managementAction: The CTO said that they shall continue with the usual way of collecting, issuance of demand letters. The CTO will conduct auction sale of delinquent real properties in accordance with Sections 254 and 258 of the LGC once the pandemic is over.

observations.recommendation: We have advised management to enforce the remedies and measures prescribed under Sections 254 and 258 of RA 7160 to collect delinquent real property taxes. Collection system and tax campaign should be strengthened and intensified to eliminate and/or minimize tax delinquencies. The City Assessor should promptly address the issue of double assessment and other data base problems as explained by the City Treasurer and properly advise the Accounting Office for appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The CTO said that they shall continue with the usual way of collecting, issuance of demand letters. Immediately after the pandemic, the CTO will conduct auction sale of delinquent real properties in accordance with Sections 254 and 258 of the LGC.

observations.recommendation: Prioritize the registration of its property located in Asin, Tuba, Benguet. It should cause the appraisal as well as the proper accounting thereof so that it can be recorded in its books of accounts as Property, Plant and Equipment.
observations.managementAction: The CLO stated that they are having difficulty convening all the heirs of Mr. Lucio Ramirez because based on the information they gathered, there is a problem in identifying the real heirs of Mr. Ramirez. Thus, the judicial action segregation is still on hold.

management consider hiring more personnel to augment the staff requirements of the Accounting Office.

continuously require/demand the immediate settlement of the remaining overdue cash advances and if necessary, withhold the salaries of the concerned officials and employees for the settlement thereof; and

observations.recommendation: We commended the Municipal Accountant for efforts made in significantly reducing the balance of long outstanding payables. Nevertheless, we recommended that the Accounting Office continuously trace the original entry made and make the necessary adjustments on the dormant payable accounts to present fairly the ﬁnancial statements. Payables maintained in the books of accounts shall be properly documented and supported with valid claims of speciﬁc creditors, processed vouchers or payrolls, demand payments or bills. Likewise, the Accounting Unit should maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for Payable accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the appropriation and actual expenses for Personal Services for one fiscal year should not exceed the required 55% of the total annual income from regular sources realized by the Municipality in the next preceding fiscal year pursuant to Section 325(a) of R.A. No. 7160;

exert effort in reducing its PS expenses by identifying and funding only PS items that are of priority concerns so that it can continue to allocate adequate funds for the implementation of other planned and development projects that are beneficial to its constituents; and

exert effort in raising additional revenues to adequately fund its existing PS costs.
observations.managementAction: The municipality was able to comply with the PS limit in FY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate prior years’ audit recommendation that management enforce immediate settlement of the disallowances and comply with Section 7.1.1 of the Revised Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts as implemented under COA Circular No. 2009 – 006.
observations.managementAction: Some of the Disallowances have already been collected and continuous efforts are being undertaken to collect the entire amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly monitor the appropriations of SEF to ensure that expenses are incurred within the threshold set. In case of a need to incur expenses which were not appropriated or beyond the prior appropriations, the Local School Board should approve a Supplemental Budget to authorize the same.

All projects were already implemented except the Labbi-Bayukan Road which will be implemented when the equipment becomes available.

The reported GAD budget and accomplishments are more than the minimum five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Disbursing Officer to maintain cashbook for Calamity Fund in which he was granted cash advances.

further strengthen its internal control by ensuring that all financial reports and forms are properly and completely accomplished to guaranty the propriety and regularity of its transactions; and

strictly adhere with the provisions of the Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs.

observations.recommendation: a. That the City Accountant provide for depreciation and impairment for the City’s assets for proper valuation and recording.

observations.recommendation: a. Submit legal basis or authority for the grant of the Productivity Incentive Benefit, otherwise, require all officials and employees to refund the amount received.

ND No. 2014-001-101(12) totaling ₱25,065,725.73 and ND No. 14-002-101(12) totaling ₱1,089,478.95 were issued on January 23, 2014 and June 3, 2014, respectively, disallowing the PIB and requiring the recipient officials and employees to refund the amount. These are pending with the COA Commission Proper.

observations.recommendation: a. Consider creating an Internal Audit Service independent of the City Accounting Office, to perform staff functions in accordance with the above provisions of Republic Act No. 4177 and Administrative Order Nos. 70 and 278.

The Accounting Office has yet to comply with the recommendation.

 require the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables and determine the concerned debtors to ascertain their collectability. observations.recommendation:
1.15. In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the City Government:
1.15.1. immediately request Squidpay to incorporate a Front-End
system that communicates through user interface or standard
communication protocols that collects the data required and
automatically issue an Electronic Official Receipt (EOR) and
to institute some urgent measures designed to mitigate the
adverse effects of the noted deficiencies. Meantime, temporary
discontinuance of the use of the Electronic Payment Collection
System provided by the Electronic Payment Gateway Provider
- Squidpay Technology, Inc. until such time that Squidpay
organizes its system is also recommended; and
1.15.2. require Squidpay Technology, Inc. to assist the City Treasurer
in resolving the issue on the discrepancy of collections in the
market arrival fees.

2.9. In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the City Government
immediately negotiate with Squidpay for an agreement regarding a
collection and settlement procedure with the incorporation of a partner
bank as required under Section VII of DTI-DOF JDAO No. 02, S. 2006.
To avoid further complications, temporary discontinuance of the use of
the Electronic Payment Collection System provided by the Electronic
Payment Gateway Provider - Squidpay Technology, Inc., is also
recommended until such time that the deficiencies are sufficiently
addressed.

3.9. We recommended that the City Government initiate the amendment of
the conditions of the MOA with the EPGP – Squidpay Technologies,
Inc., to incorporate the basic Principles of Implementation of an EPCS,
and to include the provisions on the minimum requirements provided
under Section VII of DTI-DOF JDAO No. 02, S. 2006.

4.12. We recommended that the City Government through the City
Treasurer:
4.12.1. remit to the Bureau of Treasury the balance of the unremitted
collections for Fire Code Fees as of December 31, 2020
amounting to ₱21,317,645.24; and
4.12.2. comply with the terms of the MOA between the City
Government of Baguio and the BFP, specifically to
remit/deposit the monthly collection on or before the fifteenth
(15th) day of the following month in the Bureau of Fire
Protection Landbank Account Name: Bureau of Treasury –
CAR No. 3402280416 (Naguilian Branch).

5.9. We recommended that the City Government, through the City
Treasurer, after depositing all unremitted collections of fire code fees:
5.9.1. coordinate with the BFP-Baguio City to compute the LGU
share of the City Government based on the total fire code fees
collected by both LGU and BFP; and
5.9.2. coordinate with the BFP and the Department of Budget and
Management for the release of the SARO and NCA
corresponding to the computed 20% share of the City
Government from all fire code fees remitted from CY 2010 to
CY 2020.

7.10. We recommended that the City Government:
7.10.1. handle its cash through the imprest system whereby payments
(to suppliers) are to be made by check or through Automatic
Debit to Account, or in instances when it may be very difficult,
impractical or impossible to make payments by check,
payments may be made by the disbursing officer in the form of
cash through cash advance drawn and maintained in
accordance with COA rules and regulation; and
7.10.2. require the supplier JP Acosta Trading, through the GSO, a
Sales Invoice as acknowledgment of receipt of payment for the
goods/medical supplies by the City Government, and a legally
acceptable document that would justify the deposit of the cash
payment to Rigging Trading instead of to J.P. Acosta.

15.6. We recommended that the City Government:
15.6.1 review the unimplemented GAD PPAs if these are still feasible
and update and implement the same to maximize the
utilization of GAD funds in order to attain the objective for
which funds were provided; and
15.6.2 submit within the required schedule - the PCW-endorsed
annual GAD Plan and Budget which is within five (5) working
days from endorsement, and the Annual GAD
Accomplishment Report as required in PCW-DILG-DBM-
NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 so as to facilitate
verification and audit.

We commended management for the efficient and full implementation of the identified programs, projects and activities funded by the Bayanihan Grant. Henceforth, we recommended that management sustain the same efforts in its future undertakings.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

in coordination with the BAC Chairman, submit immediately the lacking documentary requirements for verification; and

ensure that all documentary requirements prescribed by regulations are complied with before certifying as to completeness of the supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that management ensure that all the relevant and required information in Purchase Orders are properly accomplished to ensure that the supplier will deliver the goods/items in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract.

We recommended that management:
require the submission of the lacking supporting documents for further review by this office; and
submit written explanation on the deficiencies noted for further evaluation.

We recommended that:
the Accounting Office comply with the provision of Section 114 of P.D. No 1445 and Section 4 (e) of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Volume I, so that all transactions would be properly documented and recorded. With this, all transactions can be readily verified and reconciled with the amount appearing in the financial statements. Thus, the accuracy and reliability of the financial statements will be achieved; and
management consider hiring more personnel to augment the staff requirements of the Accounting Office.

We recommended management that upon completion of physical count of the Inventory Committee, the Local Chief Executive require the GSO to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable PPEs pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 379 – 381 of R.A. No. 7160 and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89 – 296.

We recommended that management require:

the Supply Officer-designate to determine the employees responsible for the lost/missing items by examining its copies of Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs); and
the accountable officers to file Notice of Loss and Request for Relief from Accountability with the Commission on Audit and attach the supporting documents required by Commission on Audit Memorandum No. 92-751.

We recommended that management require the maintenance of Property Cards to properly account the receipt and disposition of PPE items.

We recommended that management require the disclosure on reconciliation of the carrying amount at the beginning and end of the period.

We commended management for its remarkable effort in enforcing the liquidation of substantial amount of cash advances. Nevertheless, we recommended that management:
continuously require/demand the immediate settlement of the remaining overdue cash advances and if necessary, withhold the salaries of the concerned officials and employees for the settlement thereof; and
strictly comply with the provisions of Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97 – 002 dated February 10, 1997 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that management:
require the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivables and determine the concerned debtors to ascertain their collectability. If proven to be no longer collectible, request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts and attach required documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005; and
in the future, for transactions involving receivables, the Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary ledgers in accordance with Section 4 (e) of the NGAS to ensure complete information on the accounts and to facilitate the collection thereof.

We recommended that management:

require the concerned offices to conduct a comprehensive analysis of all its liabilities and available cash and cash equivalents to ensure unhampered implementation of PPAs;
ensure that trust funds are used exclusively to the purpose/s for which they were granted following the provisions of Section 4(3) of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 305(e) and 309(b) of R.A. No. 7160; and
require the settlement of long outstanding receivables to augment the deficient cash under trust fund.

We commended the Municipal Accountant for efforts made in significantly reducing the balance of long outstanding payables. Nevertheless, we recommended that the Accounting Office continuously trace the original entry made and make the necessary adjustments on the dormant payable accounts to present fairly the ﬁnancial statements. Payables maintained in the books of accounts shall be properly documented and supported with valid claims of speciﬁc creditors, processed vouchers or payrolls, demand payments or bills. Likewise, the Accounting Unit should maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for Payable accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the appropriation and actual expenses for Personal Services for one fiscal year should not exceed the required 55% of the total annual income from regular sources realized by the Municipality in the next preceding fiscal year pursuant to Section 325(a) of R.A. No. 7160;
exert effort in reducing its PS expenses by identifying and funding only PS items that are of priority concerns so that it can continue to allocate adequate funds for the implementation of other planned and development projects that are beneficial to its constituents; and
exert effort in raising additional revenues to adequately fund its existing PS costs.

We reiterate prior years’ audit recommendation that management enforce immediate settlement of the disallowances and comply with Section 7.1.1 of the Revised Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts as implemented under COA Circular No. 2009 – 006.

We recommended that in the budget preparation phase:
The Local Treasurer shall submit to the LCE a certified statement covering the income and expenditures of the preceding fiscal year, the actual income and expenditures of the first two (2) quarters of the current year, and the estimated income and expenditures for the last two (2) quarters of the current year (Section 315, R.A. No. 7160); and
The Local Accountant, jointly with the Local Treasurer, shall certify all statement of income and expenditure of the preceding fiscal year, the actual income and expenditures of the first two (2) quarters of the current year and the estimated income and expenditure for the last two (2) quarters of the current year (Article 411, IRR of R.A. No. 7160).

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly monitor the appropriations of SEF to ensure that expenses are incurred within the threshold set. In case of a need to incur expenses which were not appropriated or beyond the prior appropriations, the Local School Board should approve a Supplemental Budget to authorize the same.

We recommended that management:

expedite the implementation of all on – going programmed and funded projects under the 20% MDF to optimize utilization thereof and to ensure timely delivery of intended benefits for the constituents of the municipality;
continuously monitor, coordinate and evaluate PPAs to ensure the completion of most urgent, needed, appropriate and eligible development projects; and
observe the policies and guidelines in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF as enjoined by DBM-DOF-DILG-JMC No. 1, s. 2020.

We recommended the following:

the Local Chief Executive and concerned municipal officials must ensure that at least 5% of the annual budget of the municipality can be attributed and utilized on GAD-related programs, projects and activities;
the members of the GAD Focal Point System to undergo trainings on HGDG to strengthen their knowledge and capabilities in the conduct of gender analysis of programs and projects for purposes of GAD budget attribution; and
the members of the GAD Focal Point System to use monitoring tools such as the HGDG and PIMME in the GAD planning and budgeting as well as in the preparation of the accomplishment report.

We recommended that management:

ensure the allocation and utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to the policies and guidelines primarily embodied in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1; and
thru the LDRRMC, continuously monitor, coordinate and evaluate PPAs to ensure the completion of most urgent, needed, appropriate and eligible projects.

We reiterate our recommendation for management to sustain its efforts and commitment towards full compliance with standards and requirements set under R.A. No. 9003 particularly on the completion of a permanent SLF. To this end, sufficient appropriation should be provided to ensure its full and immediate implementation.

We recommended that the Accountable Officer immediately restitute the cash shortage by depositing the amount to the appropriate bank account or by submitting sufficient explanation or allowable justification.
Failure to immediately settle the accountability will result to the elevation of the matter to the Office of the Ombudsman for the proper filing of appropriate charges for the acts committed and be held liable for the missing government funds in accordance with Section 340 of R.A. No. 7160, if warranted.
Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer shall deposit intact all her collections as well as all collections turned over to her by the collectors with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day. She shall record all deposits made in the cashbook and prepare the RCD.
The shortage has already been settled.
We recommended that the liquidating officer maintain/retain a permanent file of RCDs of collectors to facilitate the verification and reconciliation of individual accountabilities.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct all collectors to accomplish their RCDs on days where collections are made. Moreover, these RCDs and the corresponding collections should be turned-over intact to the Liquidating Officer for further verification and eventual deposit.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct all collectors to properly fill out all the details required in their respective RCDs to facilitate the verification of collections and accountable forms by the Liquidating Officer.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct an independent and complete verification of collections and accountable forms to ensure accuracy thereof and in order to immediately correct / adjust errors if there are any.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure complete and proper accomplishment of the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms to ensure accuracy thereof and to promote accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require all collectors to render their RAAF at the end of each month. Henceforth, these individual RAAFs should be reconciled with the Consolidated RAAF to ensure its accuracy.

We recommended that the Disbursing Officer provide certification on the balance of his accountabilities in the cash book at the end of each month in compliance to the provisions of Section 63 (E), Book II of Local Treasury Operations Manual dated October 1, 2007 and Paragraph 6.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
We recommended that the Disbursing Officer ensure that all applicable accounting forms are numbered using the prescribed format in the Manual.

We recommended that the Management require the Disbursing Officer and the Accountant to reconcile their cashbook and accounting records, respectively, at least quarterly in compliance to Section 96 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
We recommended that the Management require the Disbursing Officer to maintain cashbook for Calamity Fund in which he was granted cash advances.

We recommended that Management immediately submit the lacking documents to enable the audit team to conduct review and evaluation of the subject contract. Henceforth, management should strictly comply with the preparation and submission of documentary requirements as prescribed by regulations.

a. That the GSO locate the properties amounting to ₱10,882,421.90.
b. That the CAO to prepare adjusting entries to drop from the books the unserviceable properties that have already been sold totaling ₱612,990.51.
c. That the CAO recognize the property delivered but not yet taken up in the books and still serviceable and under the custody of City Employees but not included in the GL accounting records.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO admitted that despite the conduct of continuous physical inventory, the subject unserviceable properties were not yet located.
The amount of ₱125,369.71 out of the ₱612,990.51 of auctioned unserviceable property was dropped from the books upon verification by the CAO.
The GSO reported that 87.16% of the identified unrecorded assets/items was already recorded and reconciled with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: a. That the Management to require the concerned offices to submit the aforementioned relevant documents to substantiate the recorded adjusting entry and to follow-up the request for recommendation from the CPDO and other concerned offices whether the lots will be reverted to the City or be awarded to the beneficial users.
observations.managementAction: Only 11 copies of TCTs out of the 42 lots that were dropped from the books were submitted. Will follow-up the request for recommendation from the CPDO and other concerned offices whether the lots will be reverted to the City or be awarded to the beneficial users.

observations.recommendation:
a. That the management to conduct an inventory of work accomplished by the contractor and impose liquidated damages at least equal to one-tenth of one percent (.01%) of the cost of the unperformed portion for every day of delay as required under Section 68 RA 9184; and
b. That the management to require the contractor to engage a catch-up program in order to fast track the completion of the project to avoid termination of contract as stipulated also under section 68 of RA 9184;
observations.managementAction:
The OIC- Department Head of the Buildings and Architecture Office in reply to the observation stated that the project will be subjected to liquidated damages upon final billing.
A Fast Track Order was issued to the Contractor and then Show Cause Order was consequently issued after

observations.recommendation:
a. That the management advise the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to intensify the conduct of verification and validation during Post-Qualification, such as in checking the performance of a bidder in its on-going government and private contracts; and
b. That the management avoid awarding projects to bidders who have on-going projects with negative slippages of at least fifteen percent (15%) in any of one project or a negative slippage of at least ten percent (10%) in each of its two or more contracts as required under Rule X - Post-Qualification of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction:
Management in reply stated, “The BAC based its findings on the certification of City Building and Architecture Office (CBAO), therefore, it could not have taken notice of a slippage of an on-going project other than that which is certified by the implementing office.”
Partially implemented reply and management will avoid awarding projects to bidders who have on-going projects with negative slippages.

observations.recommendation:
a. That the management verify the status and determine the actual accomplishments of the local development projects that were awarded and on-going as of December 31, 2019, and impose liquidated damages for projects that incurred delays; and
b. That the management enhance monitoring control in the implementation of local development projects including the procurement process to immediately address problems encountered that could affect the timely completion of projects.
observations.managementAction:
The OIC-Department Head of the Buildings and Architecture Office in reply to the observation stated that all development projects that were awarded were finished except those with problems that incurred delays.
With regards to the monitoring control in the implementation of LDF projects, it was informed that the designers were already involved with regards to supervision of on-going projects to monitor compliance as to Plans and Specifications with the assistance of the Job Orders hired to monitor the on-going projects.
Regular monitoring was conducted; coordination will be made to the respective concerned offices.

observations.recommendation: a. That the management review and allocate reasonable cost-effective budgets for the Plans, Programs and Activities (PPAs) in the LDRRMP in order to avoid the under/over budgeting of resources that could have been allocated to other PPAs; b. That the management continue to coordinate with the General Services Office and the Bids and Awards Committee in order to expedite the procurement of disaster response and rescue equipment and the bidding process, respectively; and c. That the management monitor regularly the implementation of Projects, Plans and Activities in the LDRRMP to maximize utilization of fund allocation.
observations.managementAction:
The Chief, CDRRMO in reply, acknowledged the observation and will take note of the Audit Team recommendations.
Monitoring and coordination will also be made to the respective concerned offices.
Regular monitoring was conducted.

observations.recommendation:
a. That the management monitor the delinquencies as reflected in the monthly report of stall rentals, intensify the collection thereof and avail of other available remedies to collect from non-compliant and delinquent lessees.
b. That the management upgrade or enhance the market database system so that it can generate all reports that the market administrator and management may need from time to time.
c. That the management conduct regular monitoring of vacant stalls to forestall the continuous billing of lessees who vacated their stalls and the unauthorized use of the vacant stall.
observations.managementAction:
Due to the declaration of COVID pandemic stalls owners were not able to operate regularly hence; extension to settle delinquencies was granted.
Upgrading and enhancement of collection database system was implemented.
Regular monitoring was conducted to forestall continuous billing.

observations.recommendation:
a. That the management revisit the AOMs mentioned above, which were previously issued and discussed with management and initiate appropriate action to terminate the contract between the City and BCBV Inc.; and
b. That the management coordinate with the SP for the immediate resolution to expedite the execution of MOA with the concerned NGOs to legalize their operation and to protect the interest of the city government.
observations.managementAction:
The City Legal Office has forwarded a legal opinion recommending to the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) for the issuance of a Resolution to terminate the contract between the City and BCBV Inc. The City Administrator’s Office has served Notices to Vacate to the BTC and the canteen within the area. The Baguio Lions Club was notified to negotiate the terms of the Deed of Usufruct with the City.
Coordination with the SP and other offices concerned was made.

observations.recommendation: a. That the Management to exhaust all available remedies provided under the laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance for the settlement of the Charges and for Disallowances without pending appeal to COA as provided in the RRSA.
observations.managementAction: No specific action recorded in the table beyond reiteration of requirement to exhaust all remedies.

observations.recommendation: a. That the City General Services Officer and the City Engineer coordinate and facilitate the immediate preparation of the City’s Local Road Inventory Report and the corresponding Road Map in accordance with the costing and timelines provided under COA Circular 2015-008 and to submit the same to the City Accountant for proper recording.
observations.managementAction: The GSO submitted the City’s Local Road Inventory Map. Inspection and inventory of Local Road Network was estimated/projected as 80% completed.

observations.recommendation:
a. Require the concerned implementing offices to fast track the preparation of Programs of Work as well as the bidding and procurement process of the unimplemented projects totaling ₱150.297 million and the completion of the 10 ongoing projects totaling ₱10.150 million.
b. Re-evaluate the 103 development projects totaling ₱12.538 million that have a minimal amount of appropriation for possible re-alignment to other projects or for possible reversion to the General Fund and address the specific problems that caused the delay in the implementation of the 40 development projects totaling ₱474.050 million.
c. Thru the Local Planning and Development Council, provide sufficient funding to complete a project and study on how to fast track the implementation of numerous low-cost projects.
d. Revert to the General Fund the projects totaling ₱18.841 million.

The City Engineering Office reported that out of 110 approved projects for CY 2018, 91 projects were completed, 8 are still with the BAC due to failure of bidding or no bidders were interested, 3 were reverted to the GF, 1 no POW, 5 on-going, 1 realigned and 1 terminated.
Management to submit list of projects to SP for realignment/reversion to the General Fund. The CEO noted that they are already clustering projects that are located at the same barangay to fast track the bidding and implementation of low cost projects.

observations.recommendation: a. Indicate the complete and correct specifications of the disaster response and rescue equipment in the purchase requests in order to facilitate their timely procurement.
observations.managementAction: Complete specification of subject equipment was indicated.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the CAO to record Notice of Disallowance No. 14-002-101 (12) dated June 3, 2014 amounting to ₱1,089,478.95 under the Receivables – Disallowances/Charges account (1-03-06-010) in compliance with the Notice of Finality of Decision dated February 14, 2017.
observations.managementAction: Unspecified in table beyond reiteration of need to record and pursue settlements.

observations.recommendation: a. GSO shall take the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties to realize income from the sale thereof, if any.

observations.recommendation: a. Advise the City Accounting Office to prepare the request for authority to write off unliquidated cash advances of deceased City Schools Superintendent Philip Flores and Merlinda Babilonia pursuant to COA Circular No. 80-122 dated April 23, 1980 and COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The CAO submitted a request for write-off for the cash advance of the late Philip Flores including his death certificate. Also, the schedule of dormant accounts duly certified by the City Accountant was submitted and duly approved by the City Mayor.

observations.recommendation:
a. Require the concerned Contractors to immediately carry out the necessary corrective measures or repairs to address the noted defects to prevent further damage and deterioration of the project. Likewise, parts of the deficiency in the rehabilitation at the Treasurer’s Office was rectified in 2016, while the remaining deficiency was paid/refunded by the contractor under OR No. 4220770 dated 8/27/2020.

observations.recommendation: a. Demand the immediate payment of due and demandable receivables and henceforth implement the repayment scheme guidelines for all loans; and b. Issue demand letters and exert more effort to collect payments from those who are no longer in the service and, if all remedies for collection have been made but to no avail, consider requesting from the Commission on Audit for the write-off of the long overdue receivables pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 duly supported with documents to prove that collection is no longer possible.
observations.managementAction: The CSWDO reported that for CY 2020, the City was able to collect a total of ₱619,280.00. observations.recommendation: a. Ensure strict compliance with the provisions on the concurrence of the SP as a measure of check and that the use of resources and revenues for the development plans and programs for the BCC are maximized and to ensure that the Local Chief Executive to enter into contracts with SP concurrence; b. Coordinate with the concerned occupants/leaseholders regarding the execution of a contract with the City Government on the use of the spaces at the BCC and secure authorization from the Sangguniang Panlungsod; and c. All efforts were exhausted to communicate with CMCI but it does not hold office and is no longer active in Baguio City. Legal remedies will be initiated to recover the said amount.

observations.recommendation: a. Coordinate with the concerned occupants/leaseholders regarding the execution of a contract with the City Government on the use of the spaces at the BCC and secure authorization from the Sangguniang Panlungsod as provided for in Section 22 of the Local Government Code of 1991; and b. On resumption after the BCC rehabilitation, the CAO and the CTO will prepare a monitoring system, and has committed to prepare the necessary MOA for future tenants.
The City Administrator’s Office stated that CMCI vacated the space in June 2016 but they have yet to settle their remaining balance of ₱20,000.00. Efforts to recover continue.

observations.recommendation: a. Refund immediately the amount of filing fees paid amounting to ₱82,600.00 and ensure that succeeding filing fees should not be charged to City Government’s fund.
