observations.recommendation:
We recommended management that upon completion of physical count of the Inventory Committee, the Local Chief Executive require the GSO to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable PPEs pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 379 – 381 of R.A. No. 7160 and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89 – 296.

We recommended that management require:
 the Supply Officer-designate to determine the employees responsible for the lost/missing items by examining its copies of Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PAR); and
the accountable officers to file Notice of Loss and Request for Relief from Accountability with the Commission on Audit and attach the supporting documents required by Commission on Audit Memorandum No. 92-751.

We recommended that management require the maintenance of PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards to properly account the receipt and disposition of PPE items.

We recommended that management require the disclosures for the Land Account in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee was able to conduct a physical inventory and render a RPCPPE. However, said report included unserviceable items totaling ₱2,147,220.07 (gross) which were not subjected to disposal and included missing items totaling ₱258,043.50 (gross) which were not requested for relief from property accountability.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 6, Page 38, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management: 
require/demand the immediate settlement of all long overdue cash advances and if necessary, withhold the salaries of the concerned officials and employees for the settlement thereof; and

 strictly comply with the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97 – 002 dated February 10, 1997 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to significantly reduce the ending balance of cash advance to ₱229,006.34.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 7, Page 44, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed; to reconcile said report with the records of the Accounting Office in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RCPI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the ﬁrst and second semester, respectively;

 direct the Municipal Accountant to correct the errors in recording purchases and issuances of inventories and observe the Perpetual Inventory System as method of recording inventories as prescribed under Section 51, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUS; and

 instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO to promptly prepare and submit the monthly RSMIs to the Municipal Accountant for recording in compliance with Sections 119 and 120, Volume I, of the MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the transactions which resulted in the negative balances of accounts and accordingly prepare adjustments to establish the accuracy and valuation thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

exert more effort to trace the original entry made and make the necessary adjustments on the dormant payable accounts to present fairly the ﬁnancial statements. Payables maintained in the books of accounts shall be properly documented and supported with valid claims of speciﬁc creditors, processed vouchers or payrolls on withholdings, demand payments or bills. Likewise, the Accounting Unit should maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for Receivable and Payable accounts; and

 prepare adjusting entry to correct the misstated liability accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to reduce the long outstanding payables to ₱250,674.50.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 12, Page 56, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management expedite the implementation of all on – going and not yet started programmed and funded projects under the 20% MDF to optimize utilization thereof and timely deliver the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Remaining unimplemented PPAs were realigned and implemented in FY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local School Board, being the one responsible in the formulation, approval, and utilization of the SEF Budget as provided under R.A. No. 7160 or the Local Government Code to:

sustain its efforts in ensuring that all budgeted projects, programs and activities are optimally and fully implemented; and

consider realignment of funds to other priority programs, projects and activities, if warranted by circumstances.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
   The Local Chief Executive and concerned municipal officials must ensure that at least 5% of the annual budget of the municipality can be attributed and utilized on GAD-related programs, projects and activities;

The members of the GAD Focal Point System to undergo trainings on HGDG to strengthen their knowledge and capabilities in the conduct of gender analysis of programs and projects for purposes of GAD budget attribution; and

   The members of the GAD Focal Point System to use monitoring tools such as the HGDG and PIMME in the GAD planning and budgeting as well as in the preparation of the accomplishment report.
observations.managementAction: The reported GAD budget and accomplishments for CY 2020 are more than the minimum five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriations. However, there was still no gender analysis of programs and projects through the administration of the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tool.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 18, Page 68, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management sustain its efforts and commitment towards full compliance with standards and requirements set under R.A. No. 9003 particularly on the completion of a permanent SLF. To this end, sufficient appropriation should be provided to ensure its full and immediate implementation.
observations.managementAction: The municipality has yet to construct its SLF.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 20, Page 73, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management monitor the implementation of all budgeted projects, programs and activities to ensure their full implementation and optimize the use of the LDRRMF to further enhance the disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The percentage of utilization during the year is 64.61%.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 19, Page 71, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate prior years’ audit recommendation that management enforce immediate settlement of the disallowances and comply with Section 7.1.1 of the Revised Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts as implemented under COA Circular No. 2009 – 006.
observations.managementAction: Audit disallowances which remained unsettled as at December 31, 2020 is ₱562,168.50.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 14, Page 60, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure that only relevant characteristics and operational requirements are indicated in the documents for the procurement of goods and strictly adhere to the provisions of the R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
   further strengthen its internal control by ensuring that all financial reports and forms are properly and completely accomplished to guaranty the propriety and regularity of its transactions; and

strictly adhere with the provisions of the Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Some deficiencies were still noted in transactions of FY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the accounting office prepare the required forms to support each transaction of the municipality in compliance with the provisions of the Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We commended the Accounting Office for their conscientious effort in recording transactions using the proper accounts and we recommended that the same efforts be sustained and exerted for future transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office continuously complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the BAC, in imposing the fees for the sale of bidding documents, should be guided by Appendix 8 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No 9184 pertaining to the Guidelines on the Sale of Bidding Documents. Henceforth, management should ensure that payments for BAC honoraria should be limited to the funds collected from the sale of Bid Documents.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairman assured compliance with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately submit the lacking documents to prevent audit suspensions. Henceforth, we recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee and the Accounting Office to ensure that all supporting contract documents are complete and submitted on time in accordance with pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 9184 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted on March 3, 2020 some of the requested documents. In his letter, the BAC Chairman stated that as to the lacking documents, they are still retrieving copies thereof from the PRDP Office in Tuguegarao City since they inadvertently left no copies for their office file.

observations.recommendation: That the City Accountant provide for depreciation and impairment losses of the City’s assets for proper valuation and recording.
observations.managementAction: The CAO has not provided for the depreciation and impairment of the City’s road network.
observations.reason: There is still no Government Accounting Manual for LGUs thus, no valuation method and depreciation matrix available to base recognition of the assets.

observations.recommendation: Revert to the General Fund the 22 projects totaling ₱18.841 million.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Require the CAO to record Notice of Disallowance No. 14-002-101 (12) dated June 3, 2014 amounting to ₱1,089,478.95 under the Receivables – Disallowances/Charges account (1-03-06-010) in compliance with the Notice of Finality of Decision dated February 14, 2017.
observations.managementAction:
The CAO justified that ND No. 14-002-101 (12) dated June 3, 2014 is of a similar nature to that of ND No. 14-001-101 (12), which is pertaining to the grant of PIB to the City employees. 
Thus, they implied that this ND should be presumed to be included in their appeal which is pending with the COA Commission Proper.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that all income earned from the use of area by Lion's Club, Inc. or any entity within the Burnham Park Reservation would accrue to the City in accordance with Section 5 of Executive Order No. 695 which provides that all revenues and collections generated from the operations of the Park shall accrue to the treasury of the City Government of Baguio and shall first be used for the management, maintenance and operation of the Park.
observations.managementAction: There was no stipulation in the MOA as to the revenues that will be collected by the Lion’s Club especially since they have rented out portions of the building to private companies which are not engaged in promoting neither recreation nor tourism.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Revisit the provisions of the MOA granting the BCVB the exclusive authority in the determination of the use of the whole PTA building and allocate only sufficient area for its promotion and marketing activities.  Any excess space should be returned to the control of the City of Government of Baguio for generation of rental income.
observations.managementAction:
The CEPMO noted that they will rescind the MOA

together with the necessary
amendments
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Establish a “Special Trust Fund” to be used exclusively for the maintenance and operation of the tennis courts in accordance with accounting and auditing rules and regulations;
observations.managementAction: Management agreed with the audit recommendation once the MOA will be finalized.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Submit legal basis or authority for the grant of the Productivity Incentive Benefit, otherwise, require all officials and employees  to refund the amount received;
observations.managementAction: Management claimed that the grant of the PIB was consistent with the constitutional principle of local autonomy and existing laws and jurisprudence.
observations.reason: ND No. 2014-001-101(12) as amended totaling ₱25,065,725.73 and ND No. 14-002-101(12) totaling ₱1,089,478.95 were issued on January 23, 2014 and June 3, 2014, respectively, disallowing the PIB and requiring the recipient officials and employees to refund the amount.  These are pending with the COA Commission Proper.

observations.recommendation: Consider creating an Internal Audit Service independent of the City Accounting Office, to perform staff functions in accordance with the above provisions of Republic Act No. 4177 and Administrative Order Nos. 70 and 278.
observations.managementAction: The creation of an Internal Audit Service (IAS) was not considered.
observations.reason: Management admitted that they are not yet fully informed on how to go about the creation of IAS. Moreover, there was no approved budget for the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to review the validity of the claims recorded as accounts payable which remain outstanding for two (2) years or more for reversion to the unappropriated surplus.
observations.managementAction: Not yet complied
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 7, Page 45, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate prior years’ audit recommendation that management enforce immediate settlement of the disallowances and comply with Section 7.1.1 of the Revised Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts as implemented under COA Circular No. 2009 – 006.
observations.managementAction: Management is still reviewing the accounts
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 15, Page 62, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Accounting Office to initiate the procedure in the writing - off of the dormant accounts as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Management was not yet able to implement the recommendation
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 3, Page 36, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The GSO exert utmost efforts to:
Locate the properties amounting to ₱10,882,421.90.
Coordinate with the CAO for the reconciliation of properties with different acquisition cost between accounting and property records amounting to ₱6,160,327.82; and
Ensure that the NGAS property numbers in the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) are accurate to aid in the reconciliation with accounting records.
The CAO prepare the necessary adjusting entries to:
Drop from the books the unserviceable properties that have already been sold totaling ₱612,990.51. (refer to Annex “C” for details); and
Recognize the property delivered but not yet taken up in the books and still serviceable and under the custody of City Employees but not included in the GL.

We recommended that management require the concerned offices to submit the aforementioned relevant documents to substantiate the recorded adjusting entry and to establish the validity, regularity and propriety of the subject adjustment on the Land account.

We recommended that Management:
Advise the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to intensify the conduct of verification and validation during Post-Qualification, such as checking the performance of bidders in its on-going government and private contracts; and
Avoid awarding projects to bidders who have on-going projects with negative slippages of at least fifteen percent (15%) in their projects or a negative slippage of at least ten percent (10%) in each of its two or more contracts as required under Rule X - Post-Qualification of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management:
Verify the status and determine the actual accomplishments of contractors on the local development projects that were awarded and on-going as of December 31, 2019, and impose liquidated damages for projects that incurred delays; and
Enhance monitoring control in the implementation of local development projects including the procurement process to immediately address problems encountered that could affect the timely completion of projects.

We recommended that the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (CDRRMC) and the concerned implementing offices:
Review and allocate reasonable cost-effective budgets for the Plans, Programs and Activities (PPAs) in the LDRRMP in order to avoid the under/over budgeting of resources that could have been allocated to other PPAs;
Continue to coordinate with the General Services Office and the Bids and Awards Committee in order to expedite the procurement of disaster response and rescue equipment and the bidding process, respectively; and
Monitor regularly the implementation of Projects, Plans and Activities in the LDRRMP to maximize utilization of fund allocation.

We recommended that Management:
Monitor the delinquencies as reflected in the monthly report of stall rentals, intensify the collection thereof and avail of other available remedies to collect from non-compliant and delinquent lessees.
Upgrade or enhance the market database system so that it can generate all reports that the market administrator and management may need from time to time.
Conduct regular monitoring of vacant stalls to forestall the continuous billing of lessees who vacated their stalls and the unauthorized use of the vacant stall.

We recommended that Management:
Revisit the aforementioned AOMs, which were previously issued and discussed with management and initiate appropriate action.
Coordinate with the SP for the necessary resolution so as to expedite the execution of MOA with the concerned NGOs to legalize their operation and to protect the interest of the city government.

We recommended that Management:
Prepare the corresponding Lease Contracts or MOA between the private operators and the City Government considering that the facility failed to realize its real purpose; and
Require the CSWDO to-
Submit a detailed report of the two facilities/stalls covering from their initial operation and up to present with complete supporting documents, re: collections, rentals and disbursements.
Turn over any remaining cash on hand balance to the City Treasury for deposit to the appropriate account; and
Submit justification on the non-adherence to the provisions of Section 307 of RA 7160, Section 4 and Section 68 of PD 1445 and deviation to the conditions provided in City Council Resolution No. 195, Series of 2011 .

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the GAD Accomplishments with all the supporting related reports enumerated in PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01, within the prescribed period to facilitate the evaluation of the City’s performance in addressing gender issues and concerns.

We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies provided under the laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance for the settlement of the Charges and for Disallowances without pending appeal to COA as provided in the RRSA.

We recommended management that upon completion of physical count of the Inventory Committee, the Local Chief Executive require the GSO to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable PPEs pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 379 – 381 of RA No. 7160 and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89 – 296.

We recommended that management require:
the Supply Officer-designate to determine the employees responsible for the lost/missing items by examining its copies of Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PAR); and
the accountable officers to file Notice of Loss and Request for Relief from Accountability with the Commission on Audit and attach the supporting documents required by Commission on Audit Memorandum No. 92-751.

We recommended that management require the maintenance of PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards to properly account the receipt and disposition of PPE items.

We recommended that management require the disclosures for the Land Account in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that management:
require the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables and determine the concerned debtors to ascertain their collectability. If proven to be no longer collectible, request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts and attach required documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005; and
in the future, for transactions involving receivables, the Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary ledgers in accordance with Section 4 (e) of the NGAS to ensure complete information on the accounts and to facilitate the collection thereof.

We recommended that management:
require/demand the immediate settlement of all long overdue cash advances and if necessary, withhold the salaries of the concerned officials and employees for the settlement thereof; and
strictly comply with the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97 – 002 dated February 10, 1997 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that management:
create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed; to reconcile said report with the records of the Accounting Office in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RCPI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the ﬁrst and second semester, respectively;

direct the Municipal Accountant to correct the errors in recording purchases and issuances of inventories and observe the Perpetual Inventory System as method of recording inventories as prescribed under Section 51, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUS; and

instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) and the Municipal Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO to promptly prepare and submit the monthly RSMIs to the Municipal Accountant for recording in compliance with Sections 119 and 120, Volume I, of the MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the transactions which resulted in the negative balances of accounts and accordingly prepare adjustments to establish the accuracy and valuation thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. exert more effort to trace the original entry made and make the necessary adjustments on the dormant payable accounts to present fairly the ﬁnancial statements. Payables maintained in the books of accounts shall be properly documented and supported with valid claims of speciﬁc creditors, processed vouchers or payrolls on withholdings, demand payments or bills. Likewise, the Accounting Unit should maintain complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for Receivable and Payable accounts; and
b. prepare adjusting entry to correct the misstated liability accounts.

We recommended that management:
require the concerned offices to conduct a comprehensive analysis of all its liabilities and available cash and cash equivalents to ensure unhampered implementation of PPAs;
ensure that trust funds are used exclusively to the purpose/s for which they were granted following the provisions of Section 4(3) of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 305(e) and 309(b) of R.A. No. 7160; and
require the settlement of long outstanding receivables to augment the deficient cash under trust fund.
We recommended that Management limit disbursements on Personal Services in consonance with Section 325 (a) of R.A. 7160 to avoid audit disallowances.

We recommended that management expedite the implementation of all on – going and not yet started programmed and funded projects under the 20% MDF to optimize utilization thereof and timely deliver the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended the Local School Board, being the one responsible in the formulation, approval, and utilization of the SEF Budget as provided under RA No. 7160 or the Local Government Code to:
sustain its efforts in ensuring that all budgeted projects, programs and activities are optimally and fully implemented; and
consider realignment of funds to other priority programs, projects and activities, if warranted by circumstances.

We recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive and concerned municipal officials must ensure that at least 5% of the annual budget of the municipality can be attributed and utilized on GAD-related programs, projects and activities;
The members of the GAD Focal Point System to undergo trainings on HGDG to strengthen their knowledge and capabilities in the conduct of gender analysis of programs and projects for purposes of GAD budget attribution; and
The members of the GAD Focal Point System to use monitoring tools such as the HGDG and PIMME in the GAD planning and budgeting as well as in the preparation of the accomplishment report.

We recommended that management sustain its efforts and commitment towards full compliance with standards and requirements set under R.A. No. 9003 particularly on the completion of a permanent SLF. To this end, sufficient appropriation should be provided to ensure its full and immediate implementation.

We recommended that management monitor the implementation of all budgeted projects, programs and activities to ensure their full implementation and optimize the use of the LDRRMF to further enhance the disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the Municipality.

We reiterate prior years’ audit recommendation that management enforce immediate settlement of the disallowances and comply with Section 7.1.1 of the Revised Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts as implemented under COA Circular No. 2009 – 006.

We recommended that management ensure that only relevant characteristics and operational requirements are indicated in the documents for the procurement of goods and strictly adhere to the provisions of the R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended that management:
further strengthen its internal control by ensuring that all financial reports and forms are properly and completely accomplished to guaranty the propriety and regularity of its transactions; and
strictly adhere with the provisions of the Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs.

We recommended that the accounting office prepare the required forms to support each transaction of the municipality in compliance with the provisions of the Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs.

We commended the Accounting Office for their conscientious effort in recording transactions using the proper accounts and we recommended that the same efforts be sustained and exerted for future transactions.

We recommended the BAC, in imposing the fees for the sale of bidding documents, should be guided by Appendix 8 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No 9184 pertaining to the Guidelines on the Sale of Bidding Documents. Henceforth, management should ensure that payments for BAC honoraria should be limited to the funds collected from the sale of Bid Documents.

We recommended that Management immediately submit the lacking documents to prevent audit suspensions. Henceforth, we recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee and the Accounting Office to ensure that all supporting contract documents are complete and submitted on time in accordance with pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 9184 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
That the GSO immediately verifies and surveys the parcels of land totaling ₱162,967,130.00 as to their propriety and existence.
observations.managementAction:
Of the total land for verification:
56% or ₱91,663,720.00 had been verified as government property
₱35,072,450.00 are being occupied by private individuals and a list of which had been provided to the CAO for dropping
₱36,230,960.00 are noted as for further research
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: That the GSO locates the properties amounting to ₱14,512,135.65.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2020, the GSO have verified and located some of the properties however reconciliation is still on process. The GSO wrote that a list shall be forwarded to the Audit Team upon completion of the inventory.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: That the GSO coordinates with the CAO for the reconciliation of properties with different acquisition cost between accounting and property records amounting to ₱39,805,087.11.
observations.managementAction: The GSO wrote that the difference was due to some items acquired in bulk which had been distributed to various employees and such were accounted on a per item basis in the property records.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: That the GSO ensure that the NGAS property numbers in the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) are accurate to aid in the reconciliation with accounting records.
That the CAO drop from the books the CEO Main Bodega that was demolished in 2016 amounting to ₱2,947,744.45 and the unserviceable properties that have already been sold totaling ₱731,305.60.
observations.managementAction: The CAO will prepare the adjustments pending the verification of the necessary documents.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: That the CAO recognize the property delivered but not yet taken up in the books and still serviceable and under the custody of City Employees but not included in the GL.
observations.managementAction: The CAO stated that they are having an ongoing reconciliation with the GSO.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: That the City General Services Officer and the City Engineer coordinate and facilitate the immediate preparation of the City’s Local Road Inventory Report and the corresponding Road Map in accordance with the costing and timelines provided under COA Circular 2015-008 and to submit the same to the City Accountant for proper recording.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Committee on Local Road Network System was created by the City of Baguio on March 1, 2019. However, the GSO and the CEO have yet to prepare the City’s Local Road Inventory Report and the corresponding Road Map.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned implementing offices to fast track the preparation of Programs of Work as well as the bidding and procurement process of the 120 unimplemented projects totaling ₱150.297 million and the completion of the 10 ongoing projects totaling ₱10.150 million.
observations.managementAction: The CBAO resolved to hire job orders to assist in the preparation of POWs. Also, according to the CEO, as of December 2019, 88 projects were completed, 9 are still on-going, 14 are for bidding and 9 are for realignment due to various reasons and are already indorsed to City Council.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Re-evaluate the 103 development projects totaling ₱12.538 million that have a minimal amount of appropriation for possible re-alignment to other projects or for possible reversion to the General Fund and address the specific problems that caused the delay in the implementation of the 40 development projects totalling ₱474.050 million.
observations.managementAction: The CBAO noted that 81of these projects had been reverted in July 2019.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Thru the Local Planning and Development Council, provide sufficient funding to complete a project and study on how to fast track the implementation of numerous low-cost projects.
observations.managementAction: The CEO noted that they are already clustering projects that are located at the same barangay to fast track the bidding and implementation of low cost projects.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that problems on availability of project location, identification of specific proponents and feasibility of the projects are resolved first before a project is included in the AIP for funding.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Coordinate with the Bids and Awards Committee in order to address the specific issues that cause the delay in the bidding process for infrastructure projects.
Indicate the complete and correct specifications of the disaster response and rescue equipment in the purchase requests in order to facilitate their timely procurement.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRMC has yet to indicate the correct specifications of the disaster response and rescue equipment in the purchase requests.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Enforce the settlement of the audit disallowances and audit charges.
observations.managementAction: Out of the final and executory audit disallowances, ₱1,000,460.40 had been settled for CY 2019.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: GSO shall take the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties to realize income from the sale thereof, if any.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO updated that out of the total unserviceable properties:
₱4,843,426.32 were sold thru public auction
₱396,630.32 were destroyed and thrown
₱176,260.00 are for donation
₱2,306,466.41 were found to be serviceable
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the City Accounting Office (CAO) enforce the full liquidation of all cash advances after the purpose had been served and demand the immediate refund of the excess cash advances upon the submission of the liquidation reports pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

Advise the City Accounting Office to prepare the request for authority to write off unliquidated cash advances of deceased City Schools Superintendent Philip Flores and Merlinda Babilonia pursuant to COA Circular No. 80-122 dated April 23, 1980 and COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The CAO submitted a request for write-off for the cash advance of the late Philip Flores including his death certificate. However, the CAO has yet to submit the schedule of dormant accounts duly certified by the City Accountant and duly approved by the City Mayor.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Management formulate guidelines or adopt DSWD policies on the grant of financial assistance under the AICS and other Social Welfare programs of the City;
The CSWDO to strictly adhere to the guidelines on the submission of complete supporting documents on AICS.

We have recommended that Management require the concerned Contractors to immediately carry out the necessary corrective measures or repairs to address the noted defects to prevent further damage and deterioration of the project. Further, we recommended that the Project Engineers closely supervise and monitor the implementation of projects to avoid the incurrence of project deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: The CBAO has hired job orders to closely monitor and supervise the implementation of projects to avoid deficiencies. Moreover, three out of the seven projects with noted deficiencies had been implemented by the CEO.
observations.reason: Not all of the concerned contractors have carried out the necessary corrective measures/repairs.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned Contractor to immediately carry out the necessary corrective measures or repairs to address the noted defects to prevent further deterioration and early damage to the project and to avoid disallowance in audit;

We have recommended that Project programmers/estimators strictly use the correct cost factors prescribed under the DPWH-DUPA in the computation of cost component for the approved budget for the contract.
Demand the immediate payment of due and demandable receivables and henceforth implement  the repayment scheme guidelines for all loans; and
observations.managementAction: CAO noted that several demand letters had been sent to some of these delinquent grantees. However, they can no longer be located.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Require the CAO to subject the Loans Receivable-Others account balance to impairment so as to fairly present the account balance as required by the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).
observations.managementAction: Account balance subjected to impairment as of December 31, 2019

observations.recommendation: The amount of ₱2 million in the 20% Local Development Fund but did not qualify as a development project be reverted to the unappropriated surplus.  The same to be programmed  for projects that contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environment management in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1.   Funds for said renovation shall then be sourced from the General Fund of the City.
observations.managementAction: The CBO noted that they will write to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for the realignment of the project.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Execute a contract between the City of Baguio and the Lions Club, Inc. upon favorable endorsement of the Sangguniang Panlungsod to authorize use of the area at the Burnham Park Reservation and ensure that the stipulations therein would protect the resources and best interest of the City.
observations.managementAction: A MOA has been executed between the City of Baguio and the Lion’s Club, Inc. however; the said MOA was not confirmed by the City Council.
observations.reason: Upon review of the MOA, it was recommended by the Audit Team that the City Government and the Lions Club instead execute a usufruct agreement which is compliant with EO No. 695. The agreement shall include:(a) the manner by which the revenues earned by the Lions Club shall be shared; (b) limiting tenants to those engaged in activities related to recreation and tourism purposes; and (c) accrual of  improvements/constructions to  the City Government in case the agreement is not renewed or in case of any violations to the stipulations of the contract

observations.recommendation: Coordinate with the members of the SP to facilitate future confirmation of succeeding contract or MOA to avoid any delay in the future.
observations.managementAction:
The management stated that they are already adhering to the recommendation.

A case has been filed by the City of Baguio against Kaltimex Energy Philippines, Inc. for its non-operation. The City Legal Officer stated that he will be updating the COA as to the progress of the case.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Fast track the preparation of documents for the ATDHC's application for LTO as a Birthing Home for its continuous accreditation with PhilHealth and/or other regulatory agencies.
observations.managementAction: The HSO is on the process of completing the requirements for the Atok Trail District Health Center’s License to Operate as a birthing home.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Immediately secure the Deed of Donation and the acquisition cost or appraised value of the donated properties and furnish a copy thereof to the CAO for proper recording in the books of accounts.

observations.reason:
The properties are yet to be recorded in the books of accounts. 

The Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Submit to the SP the MOA entered into between the City of Baguio and Baguio Conventions and Visitors Bureau, Inc. for approval/appropriate disposition.
observations.managementAction: The CEPMO noted that they are to coordinate with the SP to re-evaluate the MOA with BCVB.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Re-evaluate the validity of the MOA, taking into consideration its validity of 50 years for the interest of the City.
observations.managementAction: Evaluation of the MOA is ongoing.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Prepare the Contract or MOA between the City of Baguio and Baguio Tennis Club upon favorable endorsement of the Sangguniang  Panlungsod to legalize/authorize the management and operation of the Tennis Courts and define clearly the respective rights and obligations of the parties.
observations.managementAction: The CEPMO has drafted the new terms and conditions of the MOA which is for joint meeting/deliberation.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Revisit Sangguniang Panlungsod Resolution Nos. 106-76 and 24-80 dated October 8, 1976 and June 4, 1980, respectively, and consider amending the terms and conditions on the management, operation and use of the tennis courts.
observations.managementAction:
The drafted MOA was delegated to the City Administrator’s Office.

An Administrative Order was issued transferring the operation and management of the Athletic Bowl Complex.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned members of the SP for the immediate refund of the overpaid RATA.
observations.managementAction: The CAO has partially collected the overpayment of RATA. As of December 31, 2019, the balance of the unrefunded RATA amounts to of ₱25,500.00.
observations.reason:
The unsettled amount refers to two former councilors, Mr. Cariño whose clearance remains unprocessed and Mr. Balajadia who has a verbal promise to settle his balance.

The Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Require incumbent barangay officials who are in the list of those who have not yet settled their long outstanding obligations relative to the trip in 2003 to Mandaue City, Cebu, to settle their accounts in full or through payroll deduction from their honoraria. If warranted and in case they refuse to pay, coordinate with the City Legal Office to enforce payment thereof; and
observations.managementAction:
As of June 30, 2019, only two Barangays - Sto. Rosario and Loakan Apugan refunded the total amount of ₱22,000.00. 
The CAO stated that demand letters were issued to the barangay officials on the list who are still occupying incumbent positions in their barangays.
observations.reason:
Some Barangay Officials were unwilling to refund since they gave justification letters why they were not able to go to the trip. 
The Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Issue demand letters and exert more effort to collect payments from those who are no longer in the service and, if all remedies for collection have been made but to no avail, consider requesting from the Commission on Audit for the write-off of the long overdue receivables pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 duly supported with documents to prove that collection is no longer possible.
observations.managementAction:
Demand letters and follow-ups were made to the concerned Barangays.

The City requested the assistance of incumbent barangay officials for the collection of these unsettled obligations.

In addition, the CAO wrote that since the amount has been unliquidated for more than 16 years and all remedies for collection have been performed, a request for write off will be made to COA.
observations.reason:
Difficulty in collecting from those who were no longer re-elected.

The Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Determine actual areas being occupied by the two restaurants and submit a copy of the results of the survey to the Audit Team for verification purposes. Should it be established that the actual areas occupied/used by the two establishments are more than 212.15 square meters and 235 square meters, respectively, and then collect the deficiency in rental income from 1999 to the present.
observations.managementAction: A Memorandum of Agreement between the City and the Proprietor of the two restaurants was executed on October 2016.  The Lessee had agreed to pay deficiency back rental income back to CY 2012 amounting to ₱4,238,843.00 payable in 60 monthly installments. The new monthly rental was set at ₱160,000.00.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Office of the City Treasurer to intensify its efforts in the collection of delinquent lease rentals, including surcharges and interest. For long overdue accounts, we have recommended that the City terminate the corresponding lease contracts of the concerned lessees in accordance with Item No. 6, Section 37 of Tax Ordinance No. 2000-001, or avail itself of the civil remedies provided under Section183 of the Local Government Code;
observations.managementAction: The delinquent lot lease rentals as of December 31, 2019 amounted to ₱49,737,404.60. The City has increased the rental from ₱0.75 to ₱6.00 per square meter in 2016.
observations.reason:
Payments of delinquent accounts were on-going.

The Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Intensify efforts in the collection of overdue rentals including penalties and interest in the total amount of ₱1,136,112.32 from the delinquent stall leaseholders as well as avail of the civil remedies provided under the Tax Ordinance to generate more income for the City and to decrease if not eliminate delinquent accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The CTO stated that the delinquent market stall lessees as of December 31, 2019 amount to ₱867,438.00 

The following were done to increase collection from delinquent stall lessees:

conduct of draw lots of delinquent stalls
initiated re-measurement of stalls and coincided the areas with existing floor area rates stipulated in Tax Ordinance 2000-001
issued 372 demand letters
formulated guidelines to standardize the price of each dormant stall
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: We have advised management to enforce the remedies and measures prescribed under Sections 254 and 258 of RA 7160 to collect delinquent real property taxes. Collection system and tax campaign should be strengthened and intensified to eliminate and/or minimize tax delinquencies. The City Assessor should promptly address the issue of double assessment and other data base problems as explained by the City Treasurer and properly advise the Accounting Office for appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The following were initiated as intervention to increase collection from delinquent RPT:

issuance of demand letters to delinquent taxpayers
issuance of list of delinquent taxpayers to barangays
issuance of SOA; and
request of new server of RPTACS

Delinquent RPT as of December 31, 2019 amount to ₱49,737,404.60
observations.reason:
The non-collection of the remaining balance of delinquent accounts was attributed to the following:

Demand letters were “Returned to Sender” due to reasons such as: incorrect or incomplete address;
Real Property owners claim for tax exemption 
Names and addresses of property owners were not updated
Real properties that were donated to the City and were accepted by the council but not yet dropped from the assessment rolls

observations.recommendation: Prioritize the registration of its property located in Asin, Tuba, Benguet. It should cause the appraisal as well as the proper accounting thereof so that it can be recorded in its books of accounts as Property, Plant and Equipment.
observations.managementAction:
The building was already recorded in the books while the lot was referred to the City Legal Office for Judicial Segregation from the mother title No. TCT-T-410.

The Execution of Extra-judicial Settlement and the mode of transfer are pending while waiting for the tax declaration to be filed in the Municipal Assessor’s Office.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by management to resolve the issue

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management undertake a complete periodic physical inventory of municipal properties and immediately reconcile the balances of any affected accounts to permit fair presentation of PPE in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee was able to conduct a physical inventory and render a RPCPPE. However, said report included unserviceable items totaling ₱2,149,155.07 which were not subjected to disposal and included missing items totaling ₱185,654.00 which were not requested for relief  from property accountability
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 1, Page 27, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Treasurer, to check and reconcile balances of cash in bank per book and per bank of the Municipality as at December 31, 2018 per fund and effect the necessary adjustments, if any. The result of the reconciliation should be duly documented and a copy of which furnished to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to (1) observe the rules on the granting and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445; and (2) enforce the immediate liquidation of the unliquidated accounts and impose sanctions as provided under COA Circular 2012-004.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to reduce the balance of cash advances to ₱880,085.50 as of year-end.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 4, Page 38, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management that upon completion of physical count of the Inventory Committee, the Local Chief Executive require the GSO to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 379 – 381 of RA 7160 and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89 – 296.
observations.managementAction: Unserviceable items totaling ₱2,149,155.07 were still not subjected to disposal
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 1, Page 27, Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to ensure the implementation of all programmed projects during each budget year to optimize the utilization of the 20% development fund as mandated under DILG – DBM Joint Circular No. 2017 – 01 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The implementation of some projects is currently under suspension

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to monitor the progress of the implementation of the remaining projects still in progress and ensure their earliest possible accomplishment so that the intended beneficiaries of the LGU will be able to optimize the benefits from the projects.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to fast tract and monitor the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation:
We reiterate the prior years’ audit recommendation that management through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office:

Monitor the utilization of LDRRMF focusing on programs, projects and activities (PPAs) requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management; and

Ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during each budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities are achieved.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation
The Cash Local Treasury account has a balance of ₱62,720.54 at year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Accountable Officer:

Restitute the amount of cash shortage of ₱13,200.66;

Stop the irregular practices in handling of cash; and

Comply with the aforementioned provisions of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The accountable officer has already reconciled the shortage and deposited the amount of shortage in the account of the  LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting maintain a subsidiary record for the account Cash – Local Treasury to conveniently establish specific balances of accountabilities per accountable officer, and thereafter, the Liquidating Officer reconcile her cash book records with the accounting ledgers every end of the month or as the need arises.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary ledgers are already maintained for each accountable officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer replace the stale checks if returned by creditors; or if not, revert the amount to payable accounts. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect reliable balance of the cash and payable accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The checks were already replaced and cancelled checks were already reverted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of Section 48 of the General Provisions of Republic Act No. 10964 by not allowing its officials and employees’ to receive monthly net pay below ₱5,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Employees are no longer allowed to have net take home pay below ₱5,000.00

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the BAC and the Municipal Accountant to submit necessary documents showing compliance with R.A. 9184, Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Sections 2 and 4.6 of PD 1445 in the purchase of spare parts of heavy equipment and service vehicles. Direct also the BAC to observe the provision of the same act for the procedures to be undertaken particularly recommending for the HOPE to enter contract with the supplier to establish the propriety and legality of the claims.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the driver of each of the motor vehicle of the LGU to (1) prepare a Monthly Report of Official Travels, duly approved by the LCE and (2) submit the duly accomplished and approved Driver's Trip Tickets to support the validity of the claims and to ensure that fuel consumption is effectively monitored in conformity with COA Circular 77-61.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to comply with the documentary requirement under EO no. 49 in all procurements of medicines.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality process and pay only claims which are properly and completely documented to avoid suspension and possible disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the officials responsible in processing payrolls that its vouchers be supported with complete documentary requirements in adherence to the above cited provisions to ensure the validity of the claims.
observations.managementAction: The municipality has no casual/ contractual employees during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for management to submit to DOH the liquidation papers pertaining to the amount of ₱1,399,859.19 already adjusted in the books of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to strictly adopt and promulgate the provisions of R.A. 9003 to ensure the promotion of clean environment, and health and sanitation of its residents of this Municipality. Moreover, the management need to exert more effort so that a municipal wide waste disposal system will be available to all barangays in order to avert adverse environmental consequences.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has manifested its commitment of full compliance with Republic Act No. 9003 or the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 through the formulation and implementation of ecological solid waste management plans and programs which contributed to the protection of the environment and the promotion of health and sanitation of its residents.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 13, page 57 of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management continuously comply with all regulations pertaining to its GAD programs, projects activities.
observations.managementAction: There is no assurance that actual expenditures can be attributed to the GAD budget and gender-responsive since there was no gender analysis of programs and projects through the administration of the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tool as prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 as amended by JMC No. 2016-01.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 12, page 54 of this report

observations.managementAction: CAO is amenable to the audit recommendation.
observations.reason:
As of December 31, 2018, the request for write-off was not yet prepared by the Accounting Office.
The Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Management formulate guidelines or adopt DSWD policies on the grant of financial assistance under the AICS and other Social Welfare programs of the City;
observations.managementAction: Guidelines were formulated by the City Social Welfare and Development Office but such only stated the minimum and maximum amount of grant similar to governing rules. Specific rules as to the granting of said financial assistance are yet to be formulated.
observations.reason:
As per interview, amount of grant was solely dependent on the Social Worker's Evaluation-documents of which remain in the CSWDO file and not forwarded as part of the attachments for paid claims.
The Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.managementAction: The CSWDO was not able to formulate specific guidelines pertaining to the granting of financial assistance.
observations.reason: The Audit Team shall continuously monitor the status of the formulation of said guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Project programmers/estimators strictly use the correct cost factors prescribed under the DPWH-DUPA in the computation of cost component for the approved budget for the contract.
observations.managementAction: The CBAO pointed out that they are following the costing prescribed by the City Council.

The CAO justified that ND No. 14-002-101 (12) dated June 3, 2014 is of a similar nature to that of ND No. 14-001-101 (12), which is pertaining to the grant of PIB to the City employees. 
Thus, they implied that this ND should be presumed to be included in their appeal which is pending with the COA Commission Proper.
The Loans receivable was not yet subjected to impairment by the CAO.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.managementAction: There was no stipulation in the MOA as to the revenues that will be collected by the Lion’s Club especially since they have rented out portions of the building to private companies which are not engaged in promoting neither recreation nor tourism.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Refund immediately the amount of filing fees paid amounting to ₱82,600.00 and ensure that succeeding filing fees should not be charged to City Government’s fund; and
observations.managementAction: Management justified that COA Resolution No. 2013-016 should not be applied since the City government and its officials were acting on their official capacity when the disbursements were made and the same were with legal bases.
observations.reason: A Notice of Disallowance was issued in the amount of ₱82,600.00. The said disallowance has been appealed by the City to the Commission proper. No decision has been received as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: Revisit the provisions of the MOA granting the BCVB the exclusive authority in the determination of the use of the whole PTA building and allocate only sufficient area for its promotion and marketing activities.  Any excess space should be returned to the control of the City of Government of Baguio for generation of rental income.
observations.managementAction: The CEPMO noted that they will rescind the MOA together with the necessary amendments.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.managementAction: Management agreed with the audit recommendation once the MOA will be finalized.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Submit legal basis or authority for the grant of the Productivity Incentive Benefit, otherwise, require all officials and employees  to refund the amount received; and
observations.managementAction: Management claimed that the grant of the PIB was consistent with the constitutional principle of local autonomy and existing laws and jurisprudence.
observations.reason: ND No. 2014-001-101(12) as amended totaling ₱25,065,725.73 and ND No. 14-002-101(12) totaling ₱1,089,478.95 were issued on January 23, 2014 and June 3, 2014, respectively, disallowing the PIB and requiring the recipient officials and employees to refund the amount.  These are pending with the COA Commission Proper.

observations.recommendation: Consider creating an Internal Audit Service independent of the City Accounting Office, to perform staff functions in accordance with the above provisions of Republic Act No. 4177 and Administrative Order Nos. 70 and 278.
observations.managementAction: The creation of an Internal Audit Service (IAS) was not considered.
observations.reason:
Management admitted that they are not yet fully informed on how to go about the creation of IAS. Moreover, there was no approved budget for the year.  
The Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enforce immediate settlement of the disallowances and comply with Section 7.1.1 of the Revised Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts as implemented under COA Circular No. 2009 – 006.
observations.managementAction: Management sent demand letters to the persons liable but no settlement was made.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 13, Page 41, Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that:
The GSO exert utmost efforts to:
Immediately verify and survey the parcels of land totaling ₱162,967,130.00 as to their propriety and existence.
Locate the properties amounting to ₱14,512,135.65. (refer to Annex “B” for details)
Coordinate with the CAO for the reconciliation of properties with different acquisition cost between accounting and property records amounting to ₱39,805,087.11 (refer to Annex “D” for details”); and
Ensure that the NGAS property numbers in the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) are accurate to aid in the reconciliation with accounting records.
The CAO to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to:
Drop from the books the CEO Main Bodega that was demolished in 2016 amounting to ₱2,947,744.45 and the unserviceable properties that have already been sold totaling ₱731,305.60. (refer to Annex “B-1” for details); and
Recognize the property delivered but not yet taken up in the books and still serviceable and under the custody of City Employees but not included in the GL.

We have recommended that the City General Services Officer and the City Engineer coordinate and facilitate the immediate preparation of the City’s Local Road Inventory Report and the corresponding Road Map in accordance with the costing and timelines provided under COA Circular 2015-008 and to submit the same to the City Accountant for proper recording.
We have recommended that the City Accountant provide for depreciation and impairment losses of the City’s assets for proper valuation and recording.

Wehave recommended thatthe City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (CDRRMC):
Coordinate with the Bids and Awards Committee in order to address the specific issues that cause the delay in the bidding process for infrastructure projects; and
Indicate the complete and correct specifications of the disaster response and rescue equipment in the purchase requests in order to facilitate their timely procurement.
We have recommended that management enforce the settlement of the audit disallowances and audit charges.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the GAD Accomplishments with all the supporting related reports enumerated in PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01, within the prescribed period to facilitate the evaluation of the City’s performance in addressing gender issues and concerns.

We recommended that Management undertake a complete periodic physical inventory of municipal properties and immediately reconcile the balances of any affected accounts to permit fair presentation of PPE in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Treasurer, to check and reconcile balances of cash in bank per book and per bank of the Municipality as at December 31, 2018 per fund and effect the necessary adjustments, if any. The result of the reconciliation should be duly documented and a copy of which furnished to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation.

We recommended for management to (1) observe the rules on the granting and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445; and (2) enforce the immediate liquidation of the unliquidated accounts and impose sanctions as provided under COA Circular 2012-004.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to review the validity of the claims recorded as accounts payable which remain outstanding for two (2) years or more for reversion to the unappropriated surplus.

We recommended for management that upon completion of physical count of the Inventory Committee, the Local Chief Executive require the GSO to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 379 – 381 of RA 7160 and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89 – 296.

We recommended the management to ensure the implementation of all programmed projects during each budget year to optimize the utilization of the 20% development fund as mandated under DILG – DBM Joint Circular No. 2017 – 01 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended for management to monitor the progress of the implementation of the remaining projects still in progress and ensure their earliest possible accomplishment so that the intended beneficiaries of the LGU will be able to optimize the benefits from the projects.
We recommended for management to fast tract and monitor the implementation of the projects.

We reiterate the prior years’ audit recommendation that management through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office:
Monitor the utilization of LDRRMF focusing on programs, projects and activities (PPAs) requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management; and
Ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during each budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities are achieved.

We recommended that the Accountable Officer:
Restitute the amount of cash shortage of P13,200.66;
Stop the irregular practices in handling of cash; and
Comply with the aforementioned provisions of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Accounting maintain a subsidiary record for the account Cash – Local Treasury to conveniently establish specific balances of accountabilities per accountable officer, and thereafter, the Liquidating Officer reconcile her cash book records with the accounting ledgers every end of the month or as the need arises.

We reiterate prior years’ audit recommendation that management enforce immediate settlement of the disallowances and comply with Section 7.1.1 of the Revised Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts as implemented under COA Circular No. 2009 – 006.
We recommended for the Accounting Office to initiate the procedure in the writing - off of the dormant accounts as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer replace the stale checks if returned by creditors; or if not, revert the amount to payable accounts. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect reliable balance of the cash and payable accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of Section 48 of the General Provisions of Republic Act No. 10964 by not allowing its officials and employees’ to receive monthly net pay below P5,000.00.

We recommended that management require the BAC and the Municipal Accountant to submit necessary documents showing compliance with R.A. 9184, Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Sections 2 and 4.6 of PD 1445 in the purchase of spare parts of heavy equipment and service vehicles. Direct also the BAC to observe the provision of the same act for the procedures to be undertaken particularly recommending for the HOPE to enter contract with the supplier to establish the propriety and legality of the claims.

We recommended that management require the driver of each of the motor vehicle of the LGU to (1) prepare a Monthly Report of Official Travels, duly approved by the LCE and (2) submit the duly accomplished and approved Driver's Trip Tickets to support the validity of the claims and to ensure that fuel consumption is effectively monitored in conformity with COA Circular 77-61.
We recommended management to comply with the documentary requirement under EO no. 49 in all procurements of medicines.
We recommended that the municipality process and pay only claims which are properly and completely documented to avoid suspension and possible disallowance in audit.

We recommended that management require the officials responsible in processing payrolls that its vouchers be supported with complete documentary requirements in adherence to the above cited provisions to ensure the validity of the claims.
We recommend for management to submit to DOH the liquidation papers pertaining to the amount of P1,399,859.19 already adjusted in the books of the municipality.

We recommended for management to strictly adopt and promulgate the provisions of R.A. 9003 to ensure the promotion of clean environment, and health and sanitation of its residents of this Municipality. Moreover, the management need to exert more effort so that a municipal wide waste disposal system will be available to all barangays in order to avert adverse environmental consequences.
We recommended for management continuously comply with all regulations pertaining to its GAD programs, projects activities.
The CAO and the GSO continue reconciling their records and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries, to ensure the reliability of the PPE account.
