observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that the Agency Head require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to promptly submit disbursement vouchers, trial balances and bank reconciliation statements to enable a timely and more effective review of the Agency’s financial transactions and evaluation of its operations.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management through the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Accounting Office ensure that the contract and all its supporting documents including those required in auditorial review be submitted to the Audit Team within five (5) days from its execution to facilitate its review and evaluation as required in COA Circulars No. 96-010 and 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management started submitting copies of contract and all its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officer submit a written explanation and issue official receipt regarding the cash overage of P600,084.91. Also, monitor her accountabilities and observe proper safekeeping of collections to comply with the provision of Section 101(1) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management has reconciled the overage and the Accountable Officer committed to monitor and observe proper safekeeping of collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to continuously comply with all regulations pertaining to advocacies for the protection of children.
observations.managementAction: Management continuously complied with all regulations pertaining to advocacies on the protection of children.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterate our recommendation that Management:

Revisit the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan. This shall be reviewed and updated every year by the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board as provided under Section 16 of Republic Act No.  9003;

Implement the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan, and the identified issues in this report should be properly addressed; and

Strictly adopt and promulgate the provisions of R.A. 9003 to ensure the promotion of clean environment, and health and sanitation of the residents of this Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with some of the recommendations but the adoption of a Sanitary Landfill is yet to be realized.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 23, Page 56, Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Continuously comply with all regulations pertaining to its GAD programs, projects activities; and

(2)   Furnish the auditor a copy of the accomplishment report of the GAD programs and activities of the municipality for CY 2017.
observations.managementAction: Management continuously complied with all the regulations pertaining to Gender and Development.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality:

Only grant allowances and benefits authorized under the salary standardization law. Other allowances and benefits not included in the aforementioned enumeration may only be granted with legal basis; and

Submit complete liquidation reports for the rice subsidy payroll. Inspection of payrolls disclosed that only the payroll for the Engineering Office was submitted to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The amounts involved shall be deducted from employees payroll for December.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly monitor and reconcile the balance of receivables on disallowances/charges recognized on the financial statements with COA reports and make the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts. Likewise, disclosure of the audit disallowances that are not final and executory should be under the notes to financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Accounts are still under reconciliation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 11, Page 36, Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the following:

The Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Treasurer, reconcile the accounts, and recognize the correct balance for the Special Education Tax (SET) Receivable and Deferred Special Education Tax Income Accounts in the financial statements and submit the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to COA Office; and

Ensure setting up of the SET Receivable and Deferred SET Income for the succeeding periods.    
.
observations.managementAction: No Management Action

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management review the transactions identified above and submit all necessary documents as discussed for evaluation by the Audit Team, and require such documents on all future disbursements of the same nature.
observations.managementAction: Documents supporting check replacements have been required but not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management do the following:

Supply Officer – conduct inventory count of all undistributed welfare goods as at December 31, 2016, copy furnish the Accounting Office and the Office of the Auditor;

Municipal Accountant - prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to recognize all undistributed welfare goods in the financial statements; and

All concerned officials – strictly implement Section 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs on the prescribed accounting of supplies and property.
observations.managementAction: The inventory is still under reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Agency Head:

Require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to promptly submit the disbursement vouchers, trial balances and bank reconciliation statements to enable a timely and more effective review of the Agency’s financial transactions and evaluation of its operations; and

Require all reports (represented by ‘x’) already due for submission be submitted immediately.
observations.managementAction: Submission of accounts was not made within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 17, Page 43, Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant through the MDRRMO submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Utilization Report has not been regularly made and submitted.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We have recommended that the CAO and GSO analyze the PPE
accounts to identify, investigate and clear reconciling items and prepare
necessary journal entries to adjust the duly verified reconciling items in
order to reflect the correct account balances.

2.7. We have recommended that the City Accounting Office (CAO) enforce
the full liquidation of all cash advances after the purpose had been
served and demand the immediate refund of the excess cash advances
upon the submission of the liquidation reports pursuant to the
provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

4.10. We have recommended that:
 Management formulate guidelines or adopt DSWD policies on the
grant of financial assistance under the AICS and other Social
Welfare programs of the City; and
 The CSWDO to strictly adhere to the guidelines on the submission
of complete supporting documents on AICS.

6.12. We have recommended that management hasten or fast track the study
and the possible negotiation between the City and Benguet Corporation
so as to immediately establish the ISWMSF.

8.10 We have recommended that management ensure that contract cost
should not exceed the ABC to avoid unnecessary or excessive
expenditures.

9.5 We have recommended that Management require the concerned
Contractors to immediately carry out the necessary corrective measures
or repairs to address the noted defects to prevent further damage and
deterioration of the project. Further, we recommended that the Project
Engineers closely supervise and monitor the implementation of projects
to avoid the incurrence of project deficiencies.

10.14 We have recommended that the City Building and Architectural Office
re-evaluate the propriety of the 149 days suspension and to impose
liquidated damages if warranted. Indicate the true date of completion
and acceptance in the certificate of completion and certificate of
acceptance, respectively. Request for inspection of completed projects
should be made upon certification by the CBAO that the project was
fully completed.

11.5 We have recommended and Management agreed to:
a. require the concerned Contractor to immediately carry out the
necessary corrective measures or repairs to address the noted
defects to prevent further deterioration and early damage to the
project and to avoid disallowance in audit; and
b. direct the Project Engineers to closely supervise and monitor the
implementation of projects to avoid the incurrence of project
deficiencies.

13.12 We have recommended that Management :
a. exhaust all available remedies in enforcing settlement of the audit
disallowances and charges in accordance with COA Circular No.
2009-006 dated September 15, 2009; and
b. require the CAO to record Notice of Disallowance No. 14-002-101
(12) dated June 3, 2014 amounting to ₱1,089,478.95 under the
Receivables – Disallowances/Charges account (1-03-06-010) in
compliance with the Notice of Finality of Decision dated February
14, 2017.

14.6 We have recommended and Management agreed to require the GAD
Focal persons of the various departments to immediately conduct an
orientation or training program for the ensuing year and seek
assistance from GAD experts in the use and administration of the
HGDG Tool. Further, administer the HGDG tool for the ensuing years
in order to determine the cost of gender mainstreaming and GAD
Budgeting.

We recommended that Management undertake a complete periodic physical inventory of municipal properties and immediately reconcile the balances of any affected accounts to permit fair presentation of PPE in the financial statements.

We recommended for management to fast tract the implementation of all on – going and not yet started programmed and funded projects under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and timely brings the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended for management to (1) observe the rules on the granting and liquidation of cash advances in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97 – 002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445; and (2) enforce the immediate liquidation of the unliquidated accounts and impose sanctions as provided under COA Circular 2012-004.

We recommended that the Accountable Officer:
Restitute the amount of cash shortage;
Stop the irregular practices in handling of cash; and
Comply with the aforementioned provisions of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Accounting Office maintain a subsidiary record for the account Cash – Local Treasury to conveniently establish specific balances of accountabilities per accountable officer, and thereafter, the Liquidating Officer reconcile her cash book records with the accounting ledgers every end of the month or as the need arises.

We recommended that the Municipal Government purchase and install a safety vault to be used by the Liquidating Officer where collections can be kept to safeguard government funds from possible loss thru theft or misplacement in conformity with Sections 2 and 101 (1) of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that the Liquidating Officer (or any authorized personnel, if any) update daily the cashbook records, and rule and foot the cashbook records at the end of each month as prescribed under Section 63 (D) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer issue official receipt on all collections and funds received by the municipality in accordance with Section 30, Volume I of New Government Accounting System Manual for Local Government Units and Section 68 P.D. No. 1445 in order to accurately account for the funds accruing to the government.

We recommended that management enforce immediate settlement of the disallowances and comply with Section 7.1.1 of the Revised Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts as implemented under COA Circular No. 2009 – 006.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant properly monitor and reconcile the balance of receivables on disallowances/charges recognized on the financial statements with COA reports and make the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts. Likewise, disclosure of the audit disallowances that are not final and executory should be under the notes to financial statements.

We reiterate with emphasis our recommendation that Management require all concerned officials to comply with the monthly submission of Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that management through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office:
Monitor the utilization of LDRRMF focusing on programs, projects and activities (PPAs) requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management; and
Ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be achieved.
We recommended that management prepare the LDRRMFIP which includes projects, programs and activities chargeable from the prior year’s unexpended balance of LDRRMF.

We recommended for management to regularly withhold and remit to the BIR the applicable percentage tax due in every payments made to Contract of Service personnel of the municipality within the prescribed period set forth under existing BIR regulations.
We recommended management to instruct the concerned official to abide by the period set forth under the regulation on the timely submission of reports.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Agency Head require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to promptly submit disbursement vouchers, trial balances and bank reconciliation statements to enable a timely and more effective review of the Agency’s financial transactions and evaluation of its operations.

We recommended that the management through the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Accounting Office ensure that the contract and all its supporting documents including those required in auditorial review be submitted to the Audit Team within five (5) days from its execution to facilitate its review and evaluation as required in COA Circulars No. 96-010 and 2009-001.

We recommended that the Accountable Officer submit a written explanation and issue official receipt regarding the cash overage of P600,084.91. Also, monitor her accountabilities and observe proper safekeeping of collections to comply with the provision of Section 101(1) of PD No. 1445.
We recommended for management to continuously comply with all regulations pertaining to advocacies for the protection of children.

We reiterate our recommendation that Management:
Revisit the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan. This shall be reviewed and updated every year by the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board as provided under Section 16 of Republic Act No.  9003;
Implement the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan, and the identified issues in this report should be properly addressed; and
Strictly adopt and promulgate the provisions of R.A. 9003 to ensure the promotion of clean environment, and health and sanitation of the residents of this Municipality.

We recommended that management:
(1)  Continuously comply with all regulations pertaining to its GAD programs, projects activities; and
(2)   Furnish the auditor a copy of the accomplishment report of the GAD programs and activities of the municipality for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to:

Prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to recognize all completed infrastructure assets for CY 2016 back to their corresponding PPE accounts to correct the entry made; and

Analyze the components of the Registry of Public Infrastructures (RPI) for all prior years’ infrastructure assets as basis for the recognition of such into their corresponding PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has prepared the corresponding JEV.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Treasurer, reconcile the discrepancy/difference between the balances of cash in bank  per book and per bank of the Municipality as of December 31, 2016 totaling P2,668,186.28 and effect the necessary adjustments, if any. The result of the reconciliation should be duly documented and a copy of which furnished to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The Management has made the necessary adjustments to correct cash balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the attachment of warranty certificates and the document indicating the history of repair on disbursements for repairs and maintenance of equipment, vehicles and others of similar nature as required under Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Histories of repairs are yet to be required from all disbursements relating to repair and maintenance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Submit acknowledgement receipts, or its equivalent, on the donations herein identified to corroborate actual distribution and acceptance of donated items by the beneficiaries. This should at least indicate the name, address and contact number of the beneficiaries and the item/s received; and 

Require such document on all future donations.
observations.managementAction: Acknowledgement receipts have been submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management reinforce efforts on the collection of audit disallowances as required under Sections 5.4 and 7.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and necessary documentations on the actions of the Municipality must be compiled, copy furnish the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Management has sent demand letters to responsible persons.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 10, Page 35, Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit all necessary documents as discussed above for evaluation by the Audit Team and require such documents on all future disbursements of the same nature and comply with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Required documents have been submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to adjust the balance of Trust Liabilities-Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to reflect the correct balance of the account.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has prepared the necessary JEV and all adjustments have been effected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the stale checks to reflect the correct cash in bank and accounts payable balances as at December 31, 2016; and

Municipal Treasurer shall, at least one month before a check becomes stale, send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check for acceptance.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has prepared the JEV to adjust the bank balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Strictly comply with the provisions of Section 1 of the Republic Act No. 5447; 

Prudently evaluate all projects and activities to be undertaken if these are covered by the Special Education Fund before charging these to the Fund to avoid audit disallowances; and

Ensure that all disbursements shall be made pursuant to an appropriation law or other specific statutory authority as prescribed under Section 4 (1) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: SEF disbursements are already required to be in line with the provisions of R.A. 5447.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Revisit the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan. This shall be reviewed and updated every year by the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board as provided under Section 16 of Republic Act No.  9003;

Implement the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan, and the identified issues in this report should be properly addressed; and

Strictly adopt and promulgate the provisions of R.A. 9003 to ensure the promotion of clean environment, and health and sanitation of its residents of this Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The 10 – year SWM Plan is under revision and Eco Park is currently undergoing improvements.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 20, Page 48, Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with the provisions of Section 93 of the General Provisions of Republic Act No. 10651, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act of 2015, on the submission of AAPSI report to facilitate timely validation on the actions taken by the LGU on COA findings and recommendations.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Advise the City Accounting Office to prepare the request for authority to write off unliquidated cash advances of deceased City Schools Superintendent Philip Flores and Merlinda Babilonia pursuant to COA Circular No. 80-122 dated April 23, 1980 and COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997; and
observations.managementAction: CAO is amenable to the audit recommendation, and will comply with the request for write-off.
observations.reason: Documents needed to write-off the unliquidated cash advances were not yet prepared.

observations.recommendation: Set-up and maintain a Petty Cash Fund duly supported by an estimate of expenses which shall be strictly for petty operating expenses in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002; and
observations.managementAction: The management maintained that their working fund of ₱500,000.00 is considered as their petty cash fund, from where expenses amounting to P15,000 and below are  charged from.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Strictly adhere to Tax Ordinance No. 095-95 and other related ordinances
observations.managementAction: Management agreed with the audit recommendations, and added that they are planning to adopt existing laws on parking fees
observations.reason:
There are no authorized personnel to collect.
Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Consider regulating the entry and use of the BCC as parking area for public utility vans and other public transport; and
observations.managementAction: Management took note of the audit recommendation
observations.reason: The City Tourism is  developing a new parking scheme in coordination with the Management Branch of the Baguio City Police Office.

observations.recommendation: Institute measures on the collection of parking fees at the Baguio BCC to generate revenue which can be used in the improvement of the facility; and
observations.managementAction: To be studied/coordinated with the City Treasurer’s Office for assignment of personnel authorized for collection
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: assign security guards to secure the BCC premises and the possible assignment of staff of the City Treasurer’s Office.
observations.managementAction: The approved City Administrator’s Office Budget for CY 2017 includes the expenses for three security guards (3 shifts per day).
observations.reason: The process of hiring of Security guards is on process.

observations.recommendation: Refund immediately the amount of filing fees paid amounting to ₱82,600.00 and ensure that succeeding filing fees should not be charged to City Government’s fund; and
observations.managementAction:
Management justified that COA Resolution No. 2013-016 should not be applied since the City government and its officials were acting on their official capacity when the disbursements were made and the same were with legal bases.
A Notice of Disallowance was issued in the amount of ₱82,600.00
observations.reason: A Notice of Disallowance was issued in the amount of ₱82,600.00. However there was no refund as of December 31, 2016

observations.recommendation: Observe strictly the provisions of COA Resolution No. 2013-016 dated August 23, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Management has noted the audit observation.
observations.reason: The City Legal Office maintained that the officials were acting on their official capacity when the disbursements were made.

observations.recommendation: Submit complete list of members indicating their address and the names of Non-Government Organizations they are representing to determine if all the participating members of the Baguio Teams Golf Tournament are residents of Baguio City
observations.managementAction:
Management justified that the activity promoted the tourism of Baguio City and that the Fil-AM Golf Foundation had given ₱5 Million additional budget for the fencing of Busol Watershed.
The Audit Team asserted that the transaction should not be charged to the other miscellaneous and operating expenses. Further, a criteria should be set in the selection of player members of Team Baguio
observations.reason:
A complete list of members indicating their address and the names of Non-Government Organizations they are representing, was not yet submitted to the Audit Team 
Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Explain the discrepancy of registration fee in the amount of ₱4,150.00 between the 2015 Fil-Am golf tournament invitation and in the website of Fil-Am Golf Tournament in Baguio 2015; and
observations.managementAction: Management took note of the recommendation.
observations.reason:
No explanation of the discrepancy was submitted to the Audit Team.
Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that at least five percent of the City Government’s total appropriation for the General Fund is set aside for the implementation of GAD related projects and activities and fully implement the programmed activities in the GPB to address the various gender issues as provided for in JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Management assured that for the upcoming years, GAD Focal points and alternates  will be ensuring that 50% of the City Government’s appropriation will be set aside for the implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.reason: The GFPS with the Executive Committee  and Technical Working Group  are coordinating with the various departments to ensure that at least five percent of the City Government’s total appropriation for the General Fund is set aside for the implementation of GAD.

observations.recommendation: Revisit the provisions of the MOA granting the BCVB the exclusive authority in the determination of the use of the whole PTA building and allocate only sufficient area for its promotion and marketing activities.  Any excess space should be returned to the control of the City of Government of Baguio for generation of rental income.
observations.managementAction: The BCVB still rents out the Ground floor office to Outdoor Kinetix. However, CEPMO said that they had not issued a Certification for Use of Area to  Outdoor Kinetix thus they are still considered unauthorized to occupy the space and also unqualified for a business permit.
observations.reason:
CEPMO has not made a written follow up to the City Council regarding their action on the Mayor’s request/indorsement.
Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue

observations.recommendation: Ensure that the terms and conditions in the amended/revised resolution are strictly complied with, otherwise, consider the imposition of sanctions;
observations.managementAction: CEPMO recommended that the BTC be treated and considered as a regular concessionaire to be charged the use of area fee. They will also be required to secure the regular business permit, and the city to regulate and monitor the activities.This matter will also be raised to the Baguio City Parks Management Committee.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Consider   designating a   Deputized Collector at the tennis courts who shall be responsible for the collection of fees from the tennis-playing public and the issuance of the prescribed official receipts in acknowledgement of fees collected, and the remittance of the same on a daily basis to the City Treasurer or her authorized representative;
observations.managementAction: CEPMO considered tapping the private sector for their technical expertise in managing the Tennis Courts.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Establish a “Special Trust Fund” to be used exclusively for the maintenance and operation of the tennis courts in accordance with accounting and auditing rules and regulations;
observations.managementAction: CEPMO considered to tap the private sector for their technical expertise in managing the Tennis Courts
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that disbursements are authorized and properly supported with the required documents in accordance with Section 4 of PD No. 1445 to establish regularity and propriety of the expenses;
observations.managementAction: The management said that this is for inclusion in the MOA.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that the building is used only for official purposes in accordance with the terms and conditions as provided for the in the resolution;
observations.managementAction: The management said that this is for inclusion in the  MOA.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Assist the Barangay financially, technically or in any other manner deemed necessary in order to establish and sustain the operation of the Material Recovery Facilities (MRF’s); and
observations.managementAction:
No suitable area found for establishing the MRFs. 
The City-owned lot in Sto. Tomas will be the priority site for Integrated Solid Waste Management System (ISWMS) project, wherein a centralized MRF will be constructed.
observations.reason:
Difficulty in establishing MRFs.

Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Seek assistance from the Office of the Department of Environment and Natural Record in identifying suitable lands for the establishment of MRF’s in the Barangays/Cluster Barangays.
observations.managementAction: The City  coordinated with EMB-DENR technical personnel, but no suitable area was found for the MRFs.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Submit legal basis or authority for the grant of the Productivity Incentive Benefit, otherwise, require all officials and employees  to refund the amount received; and
observations.managementAction: Management claimed that the grant of the PIB was consistent with the constitutional principle of local autonomy and existing laws and jurisprudence.
observations.reason: ND No. 2014-001-101(12) as amended totaling ₱25,065,725.73 and ND No. 14-002-101(12) totaling ₱1,089,478.95 were issued on January 23, 2014 and June 3, 2014, respectively, disallowing the PIB and requiring the recipient officials and employees to refund the amount.

observations.recommendation: Require the recipient officials and employees to refund the amount in excess of the authorized amount as prescribed in EO No. 80 and DBM BC No. 2013-3.
observations.managementAction: No refund was made by the persons liable
observations.reason:
ND No. 2014-07 totaling ₱30,621,596.80 was issued on June 30, 2014 disallowing the PEI and requiring the recipient officials and employees to refund the amount.  
Appeal on ND issued was made by management.
Audit Team will monitor the status of the Appeal on the ND issued.

observations.recommendation: Require the Budget Officer to disclose and prepare the budget in accordance with   actual program implementation and utilization to present a reliable expenditure program. Realignment of allocation under the same class may be made, subject to approval of the Sangguniang Panlungsod for proper charging of the general services, environment and other related expenditures
observations.managementAction: Budget Officer said this issue is being addressed in CY 2017 and aligning them with the accounts under PPSAS
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the City Accounting Office to properly record JO expenditures to the appropriate expense account based on actual nature of services rendered in conformity with the provision of the NGAS manual to present fairly subject expenditures in the FS.
observations.managementAction: The CAO disclosed this issue is being addressed in CY 2017 and aligning them with the accounts under PPSAS
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by management to resolve the issue

observations.recommendation: Consider creating an Internal Audit Service independent of the City Accounting Office, to perform staff functions in accordance with the above provisions of Republic Act No. 4177 and Administrative Order Nos. 70 and 278.
observations.managementAction: The creation of an Internal Audit Service (IAS) was not considered.
observations.reason:
Management admitted that they are not yet fully informed on how to go about the creation of IAS. Moreover, there was no approved budget for the year.  
Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by management to resolve the issue

observations.recommendation: For the IPS, we have recommended that management consider other viable options for the use of the structure to generate additional income for the City
observations.managementAction: The DPWH  disclosed that the IPS will be demolished anytime that there will be road widening project along KennonRaod. The structure is fully dilapidated and the proposed occupants refuse to transfer to the structure.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Prioritize the registration of its property located in Asin, Tuba, Benguet. It should cause the appraisal as well as the proper accounting thereof so that it can be recorded in its books of accounts as Property, Plant and Equipment.
observations.managementAction:
The cost of the building amounting to ₱10M were recorded under the Other Structures account. However, the cost of the land was not yet recorded. 
GSO started the process of segregating the City property from the mother title which was still in the name of the Ramirez family. After the segregation, they will coordinate with the Municipal Assessor of Tuba for the appraisal, City Legal Office for legal assistance on the segregation of the lot.
observations.reason:
No pertinent document to establish the City’s ownership over the property.
Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that the GSO attach Inventory Tags/Stickers to newly acquired properties and equipment before these are distributed to intended end user. Aforementioned Tags/Stickers will aid to effective identification and control on the use of the government property and equipment.
observations.managementAction: For implementation in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that the accounting office accordingly adjust the books to reflect the correct balance of cash in bank.
observations.managementAction: Still, the stale checks were not adjusted and additional checks became stale in CY 2016.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 11, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that :
the CAO and the GSO continue reconciling their records and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries, to ensure the reliability of the PPE account; and
the GSO fast track the verification and appraisal of various properties and those with no acquisition cost.
We recommended and Management agreed that immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties shall be undertaken to realize income from the sale thereof, if any.

We recommended and Management agreed that the unpaid/outstanding rental/lease on properties owned or administered by the City Government of Baguio totalling ₱1,281,328.50 be recognized and recorded as income and receivables in accordance with the PPSAS 1.7 on accrual basis.  Likewise, intensify collection of said outstanding receivables and apply all legal remedies available for long outstanding receivables.

demand the immediate payment of due and demandable receivables and henceforth implement  the repayment scheme guidelines for all loans; and
require the CAO to subject the Loans Receivable-Others account balance to impairment so as to fairly present the account balance as required by the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).

We recommended that the GSO and the CAO reconcile the difference of ₱4,400,467.64 between the accounting and property records, and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the Office Supplies Inventory in the financial statements. Likewise, we recommended that the GSO prepare and submit the RPCI in accordance with Section 114 of the NGAS Manual.
Management commented that partial adjustment had been already made.

We recommended that:
the BAC consolidate similar procurement requirements and adopt bidding as the primary mode of procurement in compliance with RA 9184; and
the different department heads submit their PPMPs to the BAC for consolidation into an indicative Annual Procurement Plan (APP), as basis of the agency in the procurement of goods and services.
5.10   A member of the BAC commented that a consolidated APP was submitted to the GPPB and justified that procurement thru shopping had been the result of several realignments made on the budget of the different departments. However, she hopes that with the new system of procurement being implemented by the City, the problem would be solved.

We recommended that Management submit copies of contract and all documents forming part thereof within five days from perfection of the contract in compliance with COA Circular 2009-001 and the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
Management personnel commented that they were submitting copies of contracts.  However, there are times that submission is delayed due to delay in having the contracts notarized.  It is the contractor’s duty to have the contracts be notarized by a Notary Public.  Further, they wait for a copy of the Notice to Proceed to be approved and received first by the winning bidder or contractor before they submit to the COA.
We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC and Technical Working Group act on procurement activities within the period prescribed in Annex “C” of the IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed that estimates by the officials who prepare the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) be computed accurately so that other priority development projects could be funded appropriately.

We recommended that:
the concerned implementing offices facilitate the preparation of the pertinent Programs of Work as well as the bidding and procurement process for the 459 unimplemented projects totalling ₱693,648,612.04; and
the total amount of ₱34,073,000.00, for 109  projects that can no longer be implemented due to various reasons, be realigned to finance other viable projects.

We recommended and Management agreed that the amount of ₱2 million in the 20% Local Development Fund but did not qualify as a development project be reverted to the unappropriated surplus.  The same to be programmed  for projects that contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environment management in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1.   Funds for said renovation shall then be sourced from the General Fund of the City.

We recommended that Management:
execute a contract between the City of Baguio and the Lions Club, Inc. upon favorable endorsement of the Sangguniang Panlungsod to authorize use of the area at the Burnham Park Reservation and ensure that the stipulations therein would protect the resources and best interest of the City.
ensure that all income earned from the use of area by Lion's Club, Inc. or any entity within the Burnham Park Reservation would accrue to the City in accordance with Section 5 of Executive Order No. 695 which provides that all revenues and collections generated from the operations of the Park shall accrue to the treasury of the City Government of Baguio and shall first be used for the management, maintenance and operation of the Park.
Management acknowledged the deficiency and asked the Audit Team to give them time to study the foregoing resolutions in order to act and resolve the issues.

We recommended that Management facilitate resolution of the issues in connection with the operation of the AMHPP.
12.10 Management commented that they would like to operate the AMHPP however, securing the needed license and permits were beyond their control.

We recommended that Management coordinate with the members of the SP to facilitate future confirmation of succeeding contract or MOA to avoid any delay in the future.
Management acknowledged the deficiency and commented that a Notice to Proceed was already issued to the winning bidder on February 20, 2017.

We recommended that Management ensure that at least five percent of the City’s total appropriation for the General Fund is set aside for the implementation of GAD related projects and activities to address the various gender issues in the City as provided for in JMC No. 2013-01 of PCW-DILG-DBM and NEDA.
The CBO stated that the GAD Budget and GAD Activities are mainstreamed in the budget and activities of the different departments of the City Government and that per DBM review, they were compliant with said JMC. However, we maintained that the amount allocated for GAD activities is not compliant with the guidelines that at least five percent of the City’s total appropriation should be set aside for GAD activities.

We recommended and Management agreed to properly review the Annual GPB to ensure that this is prepared in accordance with the provisions of Joint Circular 2013-01 and that activities to be undertaken are part of the GAD priority agenda of the government.  Also, we suggest that Management should see to it that programs, plans and activities are implemented in accordance with approved GPB to maximize the use of allocated funds and ensure that all issues on gender mainstreaming are appropriately addressed.

We recommended that Management:
exhaust all available remedies in enforcing settlement of the audit disallowances and charges in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009; and
require the CAO to record the Notices of Charge totalling ₱1,430,986.51 under the Receivables-Disallowances/Charges account (1-03-06-010) in compliance with the Notice of Finality of Decision and Final Order of Adjudication both dated February 18, 2009 rendering said charges as final and executory.
16.12 The City Accountant explained that the persons liable under the Notice of Charges were no longer in the service since one them was already deceased and the other had already served his sentence in jail.

We recommended and Management agreed to a) comply with the quality standards under the Sentrong Sigla Movement (SSM) Program of the DOH and conduct periodic monitoring on the HCs to ensure continued compliance with the Quality Standards; and b) address the deficiencies noted on the Inspection Data Matrix of the Sentrong Sigla Movement (SSM) Program which were noted during the inspection.

We recommended that Management fast track the preparation of documents for the ATDHC's application for LTO as a Birthing Home for its continuous accreditation with PhilHealth and/or other regulatory agencies.
Management commented that it is in the process of preparing and completing the requirements for the application of a LTO and hoping that it will obtain the Maternity Care Package (MCP) by April 2017.

We recommended that Management implement the following:
Include in the individual project procurement management plan of the staff of  the LDHC the mandatory courses/trainings in order to competently deliver the required health services to customers/patients; and
Conduct a regular competency based assessment of staff/personnel in order to determine their technical proficiencies in performing their duties and responsibilities and to determine the needed additional training/seminar.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Health Board immediately create a Committee to prepare a written manual comprising the policies and procedures pertaining to the operations of the DHCs.

22.4  We recommended and the City Health Officer agreed to immediately secure the Deed of Donation and the acquisition cost or appraised value of the donated properties and furnish a copy thereof to the CAO for proper recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to:
Prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to recognize all completed infrastructure assets for CY 2016 back to their corresponding PPE accounts to correct the entry made; and
Analyze the components of the Registry of Public Infrastructures (RPI) for all prior years’ infrastructure assets as basis for the recognition of such into their corresponding PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Treasurer, reconcile the discrepancy/difference between the balances of cash in bank  per book and per bank of the Municipality as of December 31, 2016 totaling P2,668,186.28 and effect the necessary adjustments, if any. The result of the reconciliation should be duly documented and a copy of which furnished to the Office of the Auditor for evaluation.

We recommended that Management require the attachment of warranty certificates and the document indicating the history of repair on disbursements for repairs and maintenance of equipment, vehicles and others of similar nature as required under Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipality:
Only grant allowances and benefits authorized under the salary standardization law. Other allowances and benefits not included in the aforementioned enumeration may only be granted with legal basis; and
Submit complete liquidation reports for the rice subsidy payroll. Inspection of payrolls disclosed that only the payroll for the Engineering Office was submitted to the Auditor.

We recommended that Management:
Submit acknowledgement receipts, or its equivalent, on the donations herein identified to corroborate actual distribution and acceptance of donated items by the beneficiaries. This should at least indicate the name, address and contact number of the beneficiaries and the item/s received; and
Require such document on all future donations.

We recommended that Management reinforce efforts on the collection of audit disallowances as required under Sections 5.4 and 7.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and necessary documentations on the actions of the Municipality must be compiled, copy furnish the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly monitor and reconcile the balance of receivables on disallowances/charges recognized on the financial statements with COA reports and make the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts. Likewise, disclosure of the audit disallowances that are not final and executory should be under the notes to financial statements.

We recommended that Management submit all necessary documents as discussed above for evaluation by the Audit Team and require such documents on all future disbursements of the same nature and comply with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to adjust the balance of Trust Liabilities-Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to reflect the correct balance of the account.

We recommended that Management require the following:
The Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Treasurer, reconcile the accounts, and recognize the correct balance for the Special Education Tax (SET) Receivable and Deferred Special Education Tax Income Accounts in the financial statements and submit the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to COA Office; and
Ensure setting up of the SET Receivable and Deferred SET Income for the succeeding periods.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the stale checks to reflect the correct cash in bank and accounts payable balances as at December 31, 2016; and
Municipal Treasurer shall, at least one month before a check becomes stale, send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check for acceptance.

We recommended that management review the transactions identified above and submit all necessary documents as discussed for evaluation by the Audit Team, and require such documents on all future disbursements of the same nature.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly comply with the provisions of Section 1 of the Republic Act No. 5447;
Prudently evaluate all projects and activities to be undertaken if these are covered by the Special Education Fund before charging these to the Fund to avoid audit disallowances; and
Ensure that all disbursements shall be made pursuant to an appropriation law or other specific statutory authority as prescribed under Section 4 (1) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that Management:
Revisit the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan. This shall be reviewed and updated every year by the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board as provided under Section 16 of Republic Act No.  9003;
Implement the Municipality’s 10-Year SWM Plan, and the identified issues in this report should be properly addressed; and
Strictly adopt and promulgate the provisions of R.A. 9003 to ensure the promotion of clean environment, and health and sanitation of its residents of this Municipality.

We recommended that Management do the following:
Supply Officer – conduct inventory count of all undistributed welfare goods as at December 31, 2016, copy furnish the Accounting Office and the Office of the Auditor;
Municipal Accountant - prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to recognize all undistributed welfare goods in the financial statements; and
All concerned officials – strictly implement Section 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs on the prescribed accounting of supplies and property.

We recommended that Agency Head:
Require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to promptly submit the disbursement vouchers, trial balances and bank reconciliation statements to enable a timely and more effective review of the Agency’s financial transactions and evaluation of its operations; and
Require all reports (represented by ‘x’) already due for submission be submitted immediately.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant through the MDRRMO submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with the provisions of Section 93 of the General Provisions of Republic Act No. 10651, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act of 2015, on the submission of AAPSI report to facilitate timely validation on the actions taken by the LGU on COA findings and recommendations.

Require the CAO  to account and trace the difference of  ₱189,816,836.00  between the GL and the SL and prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to ensure the reliability of the PPE account balance;
observations.managementAction:
As of December 31, 2016, reconciliation of records of CAO and GSO has continued.  GSO has forwarded items for reconciliation and CAO has prepared the adjusting entries.
According to GSO, they have exerted material efforts for the reconciliation. They will coordinate with COA on the requirements for possible write-off.
observations.reason:
Reconciliation is  on-going to properly reconcile the records of the GSO and CAO.
Reiterated in Part II, CY 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation: Require the City Accounting Office (CAO) and the GSO to continue reconciling their records and account for the difference of ₱30,745,892.41 in compliance to Section 114, Volume I of NGAS manual;
observations.managementAction:
For the quarter ended  December 31, 2016, the GSO continued their reconciliation with the CAO. 
The GSO have transmitted /communicated to the CAO several PPEs still found existing, but are unrecorded in the General Ledger. (Donation from Commission on Population,  Department of Labor and Employment and Department of Health)
The amount of ₱833,284.26 was adjusted per JEV No. 2016-06-0088515 dated June 1, 2016 pertaining to reconciling items for properties of City Mayors Office.  Another list pertaining to reconciling items of the City treasurer’s Office of ₱646,831.67 was recorded by CAO.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is on-going. Verification of records still showed that from the total amount of ₱30.745 Million of property recorded in the GL but not included in the inventory report, the amount was reduced to ₱28.341 Million as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: Require the GSO to acquire from Donors the Deeds of Donation of the eight lots as basis of the CAO in recording the donated property.
observations.managementAction: Copies of Tax declarations and Titles were submitted to CAO as a basis for recording  of the donated property.

observations.recommendation: Discourage the practice of procuring goods and services through reimbursement basis or cash advance, unless the item/service is petty in nature.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO said Reimbursements and cash advances  are only resorted to during emergency cases or when the procurement involves emergency expenses for meals for meetings or urgent vehicle repairs. As a general rule,  the BAC issued Resolution 14-46 to allow emergency supplies for urgent needs of the different offices but limited to P2,000 only. These emergency/on the spot procurement, they said, cannot be avoided 
The City Accounting Office justified that procurements through reimbursements were sometimes cheaper compared with the regular procurements.The Audit Team asserted that the threshold limit in payment through cash advance should still be considered.
observations.reason:
The GSOIS is on the roll-out by phases, i.e., planning, procurement, etc., Users are familiarizing with the system.
Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

It is expected that the reimbursements and cash advances will be lessened.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Refrain from paying personal services and unauthorized benefits (e.g. poverty alleviation allowance) of non-teaching personnel and honoraria of the ALS trainers, maintenance and operating expenses of the DepEd Division Office and other expenses not related or included in the list of priority expenses chargeable against SEF;
observations.managementAction: Local School Board’s action on this will be reconciled with the full implementation of the rationalization program of the DepEd.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Implement fully the programs, activities and projects identified in the Annual School Board Budget in order to attain the objectives for the creation of the SEF of providing financial support to intended beneficiaries as embodied in R.A. No. 5447; and
observations.managementAction: Programs, activities and projects identified in the Annual School Board Budget for CY 2013 has been implemented.

observations.recommendation: Adhere strictly to the rules and regulations on the utilization of the SEF as mandated by law.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to adhere strictly to the rules and regulations on the utilization of the SEF as mandated by law.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned members of the SP for the immediate refund of the overpaid RATA.
observations.managementAction: The CAO have collected  overpayment of RATA through salary deduction. As of December 31, 2016, the balance of the unrefunded RATA amounts to of P42,500.
observations.reason: Some outgoing Councilors have not yet processed their clearances.

observations.recommendation: Convene regular meetings for the monitoring and review of the targets and strategies as stated in the Ecological Solid Waste Management (ESWM) Plan to ensure the efficient management of solid waste generated within its jurisdiction with the active involvement of the Solid Waste Management Board in accordance with Section 16 of Republic Act (RA) No. 9003. In addition, contemplate frequent updates and evaluation of the feasibility of the plan to adequately address the present concerns of Solid Waste Management;
observations.managementAction:
Monthly meeting to review the targets and strategies considered.
Convening regular meeting was difficult due to the conflicting schedules of the members of the board.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Enforce strictly the City Ordinances and strategies to ensure 100 percent compliance in segregation at source in accordance with the 10-year ESWM plan.
observations.managementAction:
The City is still faced with the challenging task to comply with RA 9003.  
The City Government continued to implement the “No segregation, no collection policy” and conducting IEC to barangays and households.
The SWMD conducts monthly meeting to review the targets and strategies. The following were also implemented by the SWMD:
a. Coordination with Barangay Affairs Office on scheduled Pasadang Pambarangay.
b.Attendance to Pasadang Pambarangay.
c. Coordination with Barangay Officials on strict implementation of policy of solid waste management
d. Conduct of lecture regarding SWM to the Academe
e. Attendance to radio programs.
observations.reason:
Although the city’s ESWM plan was already approved,  theCity thru the SWMB ison the process of its implementation  by strengthening  the awareness on the capabilities, roles and responsibilities of barangay officials on the City’s Solid Waste Management system at the barangay level.
Audit Team will continuously monitor the action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Advice the Chief of Reforestation and Regreening Section of the City Environment and Parks Management Office (CEPMO)  to protect, maintain and closely monitor the condition of the seedlings planted and conduct preventive maintenance to ensure the survival of the seedlings.
observations.managementAction: The Chief of the Reforestation and Re-greening section of CEPMO was regularly advised to monitor the condition of the seedlings, continuously  protect and maintain the seedling, to ascertain the higher survival rate of the planted seedlings.

observations.recommendation: Require incumbent barangay officials who are in the list of those who have not yet settled their long outstanding obligations relative to the trip in 2003 to Mandaue City, Cebu, to settle their accounts in full or through payroll deduction from their honoraria. If warranted and in case they refuse to pay, coordinate with the City Legal Office to enforce payment thereof; and
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were issued to concerned Barangays in February 2013. As of December 31, 2016, only two Barangays - Sto. Rosario and Loakan Apugan refunded the total amount of ₱22,000.
observations.reason:
Some Barangay Officials gave were unwilling to refund since they gave justification letters why they were not able to go to the trip.

Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Issue demand letters and exert more effort to collect payments from those who are no longer in the service and, if all remedies for collection have been made but to no avail, consider requesting from the Commission on Audit for the write-off of the long overdue receivables pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997 duly supported with documents to prove that collection is no longer possible.
observations.managementAction: The City requested the assistance of incumbent barangay officials for the collection of these unsettled obligations.
observations.reason:
Difficulty in collecting from those who were no longer re-elected.
Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: For the building at Asin, we have recommended that management conduct a cost-benefit analysis or a study to determine what action shall be best considered, whether to have the said building be: (a) improved/rehabilitated so that it could be utilized  for its intended purpose and to prevent its further deterioration, or (b) to have it rented to interested parties, or (c) to dispose the said building thru public bidding, considering that the lot where it stands is not owned by the City Government.
observations.managementAction:
The City has took over the operation, maintenance and security of the building after it won an ejectment case against Mr. Sinot.
For CY 2016, The City Mayor issued Administrative Order No. 129 series of 2016 dated October 24, 2016, created a special committee (committee on awards) to administer the bidding process relative to the operation, maintenance and security of the Asin  Hot Spring Resort. Two Job Order Utility Workers were assigned to maintain and secure the structure. The GSO has also requested CBAO to prepare cost estimate in the fencing of the property. Moreover, two Job Order Utility Workers were assigned to maintain and secure the structure.
observations.reason:
The City is on the process of determining the best way to operate the building at Asin.
Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Determine actual areas being occupied by the two restaurants and submit a copy of the results of the survey to the Audit Team for verification purposes. Should it be established that the actual areas occupied/used by the two establishments are more than 212.15 square meters and 235 square meters, respectively, and then collect the deficiency in rental income from 1999 to the present.
observations.managementAction: A Memorandum of Agreement between the City and the Proprietor of the two restaurants.  The Lessee had agreed to pay deficiency back rental income back to CY 2012 amounting to ₱4,238,843.00 payable in 60 monthly installments. New Monthly rental will be  P160,000.
observations.reason: Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: The City could have generated additional income to finance more projects and programs for its constituents had the concerned officials: (a) pursued their study and recommended reasonable rental rates to be imposed on lessees of city-owned and-administered lots  and submitted their proposal for the increase of rental rates to the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) for its consideration and for the necessary enactment of a tax ordinance amending the rates considering that the current rates provided under Tax Ordinance No. 2000-001 are already outdated and low as compared to the current market and assessed values of said lots, among others; and (b) intensified its collection of delinquent accounts totaling ₱3,914,293.74.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer’s Office sent demand letters to delinquent leases.
observations.reason:
Delinquent accounts were indorsed to the City Legal Office. Payments of delinquent accounts were on-going.
Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Cause the execution of new lease contracts based on the approved/amended tax ordinance to have a formal binding agreement between the lessees and the City government.
observations.managementAction:
Several dialogues between the leases and City Officials were conducted for the preparation of lease agreement.
Lease holders were   informed on the proposed increase rates.
observations.reason:
Negotiations on-going before the preparation of the lease agreement.
Audit Team will continuously monitor the courses of action undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: Intensify efforts in the collection of overdue rentals including penalties and interest in the total amount of ₱1,136,112.32 from the delinquent stall leaseholders as well as avail of the civil remedies provided under the Tax Ordinance to generate more income for the City and to decrease if not eliminate delinquent accounts.
observations.managementAction:
As  of December 31, 2016, there are 164 delinquent Market stalls, with delinquencies totaling ₱264,345.57
1. The concerned officials are doing their best to collect overdue rentals from the delinquent stall leaseholders. 
•Oct 2015- ₱897,179.87 amount of Delinquencies were collected  
•December 2014- 73 stalls were sold at public auction, ₱2,369,649.62 was collected during the auction.
•July 23, 2015- 47 stalls were sold at public auction, ₱1,606,477.55 was collected.
2.A proposal of 400% to 500% increase is included in the proposed amendment of Tax Ordinance 2000-01, spearheaded by City Mayor, the Local Finance Committee, and other concerned departments
3.Issuance of demand letters, publication of delinquent stalls and constant monitoring by this Office and POSD, CMO, is being undertaken.
observations.reason:
The delinquent accounts comprised unpaid rentals of abandoned stalls of the lessee of which could no longer be located.
Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by management to resolve the issue.

observations.recommendation: We have advised management to enforce the remedies and measures prescribed under Sections 254 and 258 of RA 7160 to collect delinquent real property taxes. Collection system and tax campaign should be strengthened and intensified to eliminate and/or minimize tax delinquencies. The City Assessor should promptly address the issue of double assessment and other data base problems as explained by the City Treasurer and properly advise the Accounting Office for appropriate adjustments in the books of accounts.

The Audit Team recommended that Management submit a copy of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment as of December 31, 2015 to the Office of the Auditor and we also recommend to reconcile the counted municipal properties with the subsidiary ledgers in order that the financial statements reflect the true value of existing municipal properties at hand as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Management already submitted the RPCPPE as of December 31, 2015.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that the Accountable Officers reconcile their cash book records with the Accounting ledgers at least quarterly. Discrepancies identified should be adjusted accordingly to come up with updated and more accurate and reliable reports.
observations.managementAction: The Management conducted quarterly reconciliation of cash book records with accounting ledgers.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that Management send demand letters from previous officers and employees to settle their cash advances immediately.
observations.managementAction: As per Agency Action Plan, demand letters were served.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that management prepare projected computations for the terms of the full settlement of the disallowances and increase the amount to be deducted from salaries and other benefits of the persons liable to improve settlement of the disallowances.
observations.managementAction: As per Agency Action Plan, there is partial collection of disallowances however, necessary documents to corroborate all payments already made were not submitted to update our records and for the issuance of Notices of Settlement of Suspencions, Disallowances and Charges.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 6, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that 30% of the LDRRMF be spent strictly in accordance with the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1.
observations.managementAction: The Management prudently implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that the municipality follow the procedures laid down in RA 9184 for negotiated procurements in the succeeding procurements in cases where the municipality will resort to negotiated procurement.
observations.managementAction: The Management prudently implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that management submit the lacking documents to comply to the documentary requirements prescribed by the abovementioned circular and to avoid issuance of Notices of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: The Management prudently implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that the Municipality adopt and promulgate the provisions of RA 9003 to ensure the promotion of health, hygiene and sanitation of the residents of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has not fully implemented programs to comply with and enforce Republic Act No. 9003.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 14, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant require the Disbursing Officer to attach the documents necessary for cash advances for payroll, allowances, honoraria and other similar emoluments before processing the same so that the amount drawn is equal to the net pay for the period covered.
observations.managementAction: Some disbursements for the grant of cash advances were not supported by required documents.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that management ensure the implementation of all programmed projects during each budget year to optimize the utilization of the 20% development fund as mandated under DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: Unimplemented prior year projects were implemented during the CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: The Audit Team recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local Finance Committee to obligate, remit and record the maximum monthly government contribution to the Pag-IBIG Fund of P100.00 per employee in compliance with Section 7 of RA 9679.
observations.managementAction: The LGU agreed to match the members’ increased contribution relative to the provisions of Rule VI, Section 1, Paragraph 3 of the IRR of R.A. 9679.
